1: # GitLab
2: 
3: ## Canonical source
4: 
5: The canonical source of GitLab where all development takes place is [hosted on GitLab.com](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab).
6: 
7: If you wish to clone a copy of GitLab without proprietary code, you can use the read-only mirror of GitLab located at https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-foss/. However, please do not submit any issues and/or merge requests to that project.
8: 
9: ## Free trial
10: 
11: You can request a free trial of GitLab Ultimate [on our website](https://about.gitlab.com/free-trial/).
12: 
13: ## Open source software to collaborate on code
14: 
15: To see how GitLab looks please see the [features page on our website](https://about.gitlab.com/features/).
16: 
17: - Manage Git repositories with fine grained access controls that keep your code secure
18: - Perform code reviews and enhance collaboration with merge requests
19: - Complete continuous integration (CI) and continuous deployment/delivery (CD) pipelines to build, test, and deploy your applications
20: - Each project can also have an issue tracker, issue board, and a wiki
21: - Used by more than 100,000 organizations, GitLab is the most popular solution to manage Git repositories on-premises
22: - Open-core, most of the code is MIT with the files in `/ee` being proprietary but source available and open for contributions.
23: 
24: ## Editions
25: 
26: There are three editions of GitLab:
27: 
28: - GitLab Community Edition (CE) is available freely under the MIT Expat license.
29: - GitLab Enterprise Edition (EE) includes [extra features](https://about.gitlab.com/pricing/#compare-options) that are more useful for organizations with more than 100 users. To use EE and get official support please [become a subscriber](https://about.gitlab.com/pricing/).
30: - JiHu Edition (JH) tailored specifically for the [Chinese market](https://about.gitlab.cn/).
31: 
32: ## Licensing
33: 
34: See the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for licensing information as it pertains to
35: files in this repository.
36: 
37: ## Hiring
38: 
39: We are hiring developers, support engineers, and production engineers all the time, please see our [jobs page](https://about.gitlab.com/jobs/).
40: 
41: ## Website
42: 
43: On [about.gitlab.com](https://about.gitlab.com/) you can find more information about:
44: 
45: - [Subscriptions](https://about.gitlab.com/pricing/)
46: - [Professional Services](https://about.gitlab.com/services/)
47: - [Community](https://about.gitlab.com/community/)
48: - [Hosted GitLab.com](https://about.gitlab.com/gitlab-com/) use GitLab as a free service
49: - [GitLab Enterprise Edition](https://about.gitlab.com/features/#enterprise) with additional features aimed at larger organizations.
50: - [GitLab CI](https://about.gitlab.com/solutions/continuous-integration/) a continuous integration (CI) server that is easy to integrate with GitLab.
51: 
52: ## Requirements
53: 
54: Please see the [requirements documentation](doc/install/requirements.md) for system requirements and more information about the supported operating systems.
55: 
56: ## Installation
57: 
58: The recommended way to install GitLab is with the [Omnibus packages](https://docs.gitlab.com/install/package/) on our package server.
59: Compared to [a self-compiled installation](https://docs.gitlab.com/install/self_compiled/), this is faster and less error prone.
60: Just select your operating system, download the respective package (Debian or RPM) and install it using the system's package manager.

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequest < ApplicationRecord
4:   extend Gitlab::Cache::RequestCache
5: 
6:   include AtomicInternalId
7:   include IidRoutes
8:   include Issuable
9:   include Noteable
10:   include Referable
11:   include Presentable
12:   include TimeTrackable
13:   include ManualInverseAssociation

<skill_content name="gitlab-coding-principles">
# Skill: gitlab-coding-principles

# Load Project Principles

Evaluate ALL groups below and load principles from EVERY group that applies to
your task. Most tasks span multiple groups (e.g., a model change may need
Backend, Database, and Testing principles). DO NOT stop after the first group.
When your task involves database queries, scopes, or data access patterns,
ALWAYS load Database principles regardless of which files you are editing.

**Database:**
- **Database fundamentals (model queries, batching, sharding, partitioning, N+1)**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md *(load for any database work)*
- **Database migration patterns and zero-downtime safety**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/database-migrations.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md)*
- **Column types, constraints, indexes, naming conventions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/database-schema.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md)*
- **SQL performance, transactions, batching**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/database-queries.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md)*
- **ClickHouse database concerns**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/clickhouse.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md)*

**Cells:**
- **Cells architecture sharding keys, organization data ownership, routable requests, and cell isolation when adding tables or customer-owned resources**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/cells-fundamentals.md *(load for any cells work)*
- **Cells globally-unique attribute claims (Cells::Claimable), claim rollout lifecycle, and feature flags for cross-cell uniqueness**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/cells-claims.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/cells-fundamentals.md)*

**Security:**
- **Security vulnerabilities and secure coding**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/security.md
- **Authentication, authorization, token handling, OAuth, SAML, identity linking, composite identity, session management, 2FA, MFA, password management**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/authentication.md

**Permissions:**
- **Where to check permissions, naming conventions, role definitions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-fundamentals.md *(load for any permissions work)*
- **Granular PAT (GPAT) compliance for GraphQL types and mutations**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-graphql-gpat.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-fundamentals.md)*
- **Granular PAT (GPAT) compliance for REST endpoints and job tokens**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-rest-gpat.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-fundamentals.md)*

**Code Review:**
- **General code review practices and acceptance checklist**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/code-review.md

**Backend:**
- **Ruby/Rails style, logging, common pitfalls**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ruby.md *(load for any backend work)*
- **Service patterns, abstractions, design conventions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/backend-architecture.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ruby.md)*
- **EE/CE separation, licensing, code placement**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ee.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ruby.md)*

**API:**
- **REST API design and conventions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/rest-api.md
- **GraphQL API design and conventions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/graphql.md

**Workers:**
- **Sidekiq worker design and reliability**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/workers.md

**CI/CD:**
- **GitLab CI/CD internals — adding pipeline configuration keywords (Ci::Config::Entry classes, feature-flag gating, JSON schema) and creating CI routing/partitioned tables. NOTE this is about GitLab's own CI implementation, not authoring a project's .gitlab-ci.yml**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/cicd-internals.md

**Frontend:**
- **Vue.js components, state management, patterns**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-vue.md *(load for any frontend work)*
- **CSS/SCSS, Tailwind, dark mode**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-style.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-vue.md)*
- **HAML templates, ViewComponents, Pajamas**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-haml.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-vue.md)*
- **Frontend accessibility patterns and requirements**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-a11y.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-vue.md)*

**Testing:**
- **RSpec patterns, factories, shared examples**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/qa-rspec.md
- **Jest, jsdom, Vue Test Utils patterns**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/qa-jest.md

**Performance:**
- **Performance and scalability**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/performance.md

**Documentation:**
- **Documentation style and completeness**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/documentation.md

**Feature Flags:**
- **Feature flag usage and lifecycle**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/feature-flags.md

**Analytics:**
- **Analytics instrumentation and metrics**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/analytics.md

- **Code style or linting**: Read .ai/code-style.md
- **Git, commits, or branches**: Read .ai/git.md
- **CI/CD pipelines or `.gitlab-ci.yml`**: Read .ai/ci-cd.md

203:   state_machine :state_id, initial: :opened, initialize: false do
204:     event :close do
205:       transition [:opened] => :closed
206:     end
207: 
208:     event :mark_as_merged do
209:       transition [:opened, :locked] => :merged
210:     end
211: 
212:     event :reopen do

214:     end
215: 
216:     event :lock_mr do

218:     end

225:       merge_request.merge_jid = nil
226:     end

234:       merge_request.metrics.first_contribution = true if merge_request.first_contribution?
235:     end

238:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
239:         UpdateHeadPipelineForMergeRequestWorker.perform_async(merge_request.id)
240:       end
241:     end

243:     state :opened, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:opened]
244:     state :closed, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:closed]
245:     state :merged, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:merged]
246:     state :locked, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:locked]
247:   end
248: 
249:   # NOTE: If you modify this state machine (transitions or callbacks), you must also
250:   # review and update the batch method below (batch_mark_as_unchecked).
251:   # Batch methods bypass callbacks for performance, so any new callbacks may need
252:   # to be handled manually.
253:   #
254:   # See: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/work_items/546431
255:   state_machine :merge_status, initial: :unchecked do
256:     event :mark_as_preparing do
257:       transition [:unchecked, :can_be_merged] => :preparing
258:     end
259: 
260:     event :mark_as_unchecked do
261:       transition [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking] => :unchecked
262:       transition [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged_recheck
263:     end
264: 
265:     event :mark_as_checking do
266:       transition unchecked: :checking
267:       transition cannot_be_merged_recheck: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
268:     end
269: 
270:     event :mark_as_mergeable do
271:       transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :can_be_merged
272:     end
273: 
274:     event :mark_as_unmergeable do
275:       transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged
276:     end
277: 
278:     state :preparing
279:     state :unchecked
280:     state :cannot_be_merged_recheck
281:     state :checking
282:     state :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
283:     state :can_be_merged
284:     state :cannot_be_merged
285: 
286:     around_transition do |merge_request, transition, block|
287:       Gitlab::Timeless.timeless(merge_request, &block)
288:     end
289: 
290:     after_transition any => [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :can_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged] do |merge_request, transition|
291:       next if merge_request.skip_merge_status_trigger
292: 
293:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
294:         GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
295:       end
296:     end
297: 
298:     after_transition any => :can_be_merged do |merge_request, transition|
299:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
300:         # We find it instead of reloading in order not to clear any other attributes
301:         next unless MergeRequest.where(id: merge_request.id, auto_merge_enabled: true).exists?
302: 
303:         AutoMergeProcessWorker.perform_async({ 'merge_request_id' => merge_request.id })
304:       end
305:     end
306: 
307:     # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ServiceClass
308:     after_transition [:unchecked, :checking] => :cannot_be_merged do |merge_request, _|
309:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
310:         next unless merge_request.notify_conflict?
311: 
312:         publish_code_conflict_event
313:         NotificationService.new.merge_request_unmergeable(merge_request)
314:         TodoService.new.merge_request_became_unmergeable(merge_request)
315:       end
316:     end
317:     # rubocop: enable CodeReuse/ServiceClass
318: 
319:     def check_state?(merge_status)
320:       [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking].include?(merge_status.to_sym)
321:     end
322: 
323:     # rubocop: disable Style/SymbolProc
324:     before_transition { |merge_request| merge_request.enable_transitioning }
325: 
326:     after_transition { |merge_request| merge_request.disable_transitioning }
327:     # rubocop: enable Style/SymbolProc
328:   end
329: 
330:   # NOTE: Batch transition merge_status to unchecked/cannot_be_merged_recheck.
331:   # This bypasses state machine callbacks for performance but mimics their behavior.
332:   #
333:   # Transitions mirror the `mark_as_unchecked` event defined above.
334:   # Process batch updates in batches as there can be many target merge requests.
335:   def self.batch_mark_as_unchecked(mr_ids, mrs_to_trigger: [], batch_size: 1000)
336:     return if mr_ids.blank?
337: 
338:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
339:       where(id: batch_ids)
340:         .where(merge_status: [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking])
341:         .update_all(merge_status: :unchecked)
342: 
343:       where(id: batch_ids)
344:         .where(merge_status: [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking])
345:         .update_all(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_recheck)
346:     end
347: 
348:     mrs_to_trigger.each do |mr|
349:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(mr.reset)
350:     end
351:   end
352: 
353:   # NOTE: Batch transition merge_status to checking/cannot_be_merged_rechecking.
354:   # This bypasses state machine callbacks for performance but mimics their behavior.
355:   #
356:   # Transitions mirror the `mark_as_checking` event defined above.
357:   def self.batch_mark_as_checking(mr_ids, batch_size: 1000)
358:     return if mr_ids.blank?
359: 
360:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
361:       where(id: batch_ids)
362:         .where(merge_status: :unchecked)
363:         .update_all(merge_status: :checking)
364: 
365:       where(id: batch_ids)
366:         .where(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_recheck)
367:         .update_all(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking)
368:     end
369:   end
370: 
371:   def self.batch_clear_merge_error(mr_ids, batch_size: 1000)
372:     return if mr_ids.blank?
373: 
374:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
375:       where(id: batch_ids)
376:         .where.not(merge_error: nil)
377:         .update_all(merge_error: nil)
378:     end
379:   end
380: 
381:   # Returns current merge_status except it returns `cannot_be_merged_rechecking` as `checking`
382:   # to avoid exposing unnecessary internal state
383:   def public_merge_status
384:     cannot_be_merged_rechecking? || preparing? ? 'checking' : merge_status
385:   end

2: source_checksum: ea3e1208f5193137
3: distilled_at_sha: 4bdca94fd505e9510cf535c34f2343e7b91332fe

5: <!-- Auto-generated from docs.gitlab.com by gitlab-ai-principles-distiller — do not edit manually -->
6: 
7: > **Prerequisite:** If you haven't already, also read .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ruby.md - it contains foundational rules that apply to all backend work.
8: 
9: # Backend Architecture Principles
10: 
11: ## Checklist
12: 
13: ### Abstraction Layer Usage
14: 
15: - DO NOT use high-level Finder classes (e.g., `ProjectsFinder`) inside other Finders; use the underlying query primitives directly
16: - DO NOT call `ActiveRecord` methods (e.g., `where`, `find_by`) directly from controllers, API endpoints, service classes, finders, presenters, or serializers — only from model class/instance methods
17: - DO NOT use Presenters or Serializers inside Service classes
18: - DO NOT use Service classes, Presenters, Serializers, or Workers inside Finders
19: - DO NOT use service classes, presenters, or serializers inside model class/instance methods
20: - DO NOT invoke a Worker directly with `SomeWorker.new.perform`; use `SomeWorker.perform_async` or `SomeWorker.perform_in`
21: - DO NOT execute database queries in views; move all data retrieval into the controller or a presenter and pass the result as an instance variable
22: - DO NOT put business logic in views; extract conditionals that evaluate model state beyond `nil?`, `present?`, or boolean attribute checks into a helper, presenter, or ViewComponent
23: - Use the abstraction table to verify that each layer only calls permitted abstractions before approving cross-layer calls
24: 
25: ### Service Classes
26: 
27: - Ensure service class initializer takes the acted-upon model as the first positional argument
28: - Ensure service classes that act on behalf of a user include `current_user:` as a keyword argument
29: - Ensure the `#execute` method takes no arguments (all data passed via initializer)
30: - Ensure `#execute` returns a `ServiceResponse` object when a return value is needed
31: - Use `ServiceResponse.success` / `ServiceResponse.error` with a `message:` and optional `payload:` or `reason:`
32: - Use domain-specific `reason:` symbols in `ServiceResponse.error` (e.g., `:job_not_retriable`, `:duplicate_package`); use Rails HTTP status symbols only for common failures like `:not_found` or `:forbidden`
33: - Inherit from `BaseContainerService`, `BaseProjectService`, or `BaseGroupService` where appropriate
34: - DO NOT put business logic that changes application state in objects that are not service classes (use finders or value objects for read-only operations)
35: 
36: ### Finders
37: 
38: - Ensure finder `#execute` methods return `ActiveRecord::Relation`; add exceptions to `spec/support/finder_collection_allowlist.yml` only when necessary
39: - DO NOT reuse other finders inside a finder
40: 
41: ### Naming and Ubiquitous Language
42: 
43: - Use ubiquitous language from the product/user documentation instead of CRUD terminology when naming service classes and domain objects (e.g., `Epic::AddExistingIssueService` not `EpicIssues::CreateService`)
44: - Use CRUD names only when they match the existing ubiquitous language and are unambiguous (e.g., `Projects::CreateService`)
45: - Ensure new classes and database tables use ubiquitous language; use `self.table_name=` when model name diverges from table name
46: 
47: ### Bounded Contexts and Namespaces
48: 
49: - Ensure every Ruby class is nested inside a top-level namespace present in `config/bounded_contexts.yml`; resolve `Gitlab/BoundedContexts` RuboCop offenses by nesting into an existing context
50: - DO NOT nest feature-specific classes under `Projects::` or `Groups::` namespaces unless the concept is strictly about projects or groups themselves
51: - DO NOT use stage or group names as bounded context namespaces (feature categories can be reassigned)
52: - Define event classes and publish events within the same bounded context (top-level Ruby namespace) where the triggering feature lives
53: - Place FOSS event subscriptions in `lib/gitlab/bounded_contexts/subscriptions/[context]_subscriptions.rb`; EE-only in `ee/lib/gitlab/event_store/subscriptions/[context]_subscriptions.rb`
54: 
55: ### Omniscient Classes
56: 
57: - DO NOT add new methods or data to omniscient classes (`Project`, `User`, `MergeRequest`, `Ci::Pipeline`, or any class >1000 LOC); create a dedicated class instead
58: - Prefer thin domain wrapper objects (e.g., `AntiAbuse::UserTrustScore.new(user)`) over adding methods to large models
59: - Use dependency inversion to encapsulate related behavior in a bounded context rather than adding it to a shared model
60: 
61: ### Use-Case-Oriented Design
62: 
63: - DO NOT reuse a single service class for radically different use cases with different permissions, preconditions, or side-effects; create separate service classes per use case
64: - Ensure each service class enforces a single, cohesive set of permissions and parameters
65: 
66: ### EventStore
67: 
68: - Name events in past tense: `<DomainObject><Action>Event` (e.g., `Ci::PipelineCreatedEvent`, not `Ci::CreatePipelineEvent`); elide the domain object when obvious from the bounded context (e.g., `MergeRequest::ApprovedEvent` not `MergeRequest::MergeRequestApprovedEvent`)
69: - Define event schemas as valid JSON Schema; mark unique identifiers as `required` and all other properties as optional
70: - Publish only properties needed by subscribers; DO NOT tailor the payload to a specific subscriber
71: - Dispatch events from service classes; use model state machine transitions as an exception, not `ActiveRecord` callbacks
72: - DO NOT publish events about domain objects outside your bounded context
73: - Introduce new Sidekiq subscriber workers in a prior deployment (or behind a feature flag) before registering the subscription
74: - Use conditional dispatch (`if:` lambda) only for cheap synchronous checks; handle complex conditions inside `handle_event`
75: - Follow the multi-rollout process when renaming events, adding required properties, or removing properties (expand → migrate → contract across separate milestones)
76: - Use `publish_event` RSpec matcher to test publishers; use `it_behaves_like 'subscribes to event'` shared example to test subscribers
77: - Define CE events and publish them in CE code; define EE events and publish them in EE code; subscribers may cross CE/EE boundaries
78: - DO NOT use EventStore when logic must run synchronously as part of the main business transaction rather than as a side-effect
79: - Use `Gitlab::EventStore.publish_group` when publishing multiple events of the same type in a single transaction to reduce Sidekiq load; configure `group_size` on the subscription as needed
80: 
81: ### Modules and Instance Variables
82: 
83: - DO NOT share instance variables across multiple mixed-in modules; keep instance variables contained within the module that owns them
84: - Prefer the `@var ||= value` single-assignment pattern when memoizing in a module
85: - DO NOT use instance variables in view partials; pass locals explicitly and fetch with `local_assigns.fetch(:key)`
86: 
87: ### Abstract Methods
88: 
89: - Use `Gitlab::AbstractMethodError` (not `NotImplementedError`, `NoMethodError`, or a generic string `raise`) for abstract methods that subclasses must implement
90: - Prefer composition and duck typing over inheritance; use abstract methods only for framework integration points or shared-template components (e.g., ViewComponents)
91: 
92: ### Application Limits
93: 
94: - Add new plan limits as a column in `plan_limits` with a non-null default, then fine-tune per plan using `create_or_update_plan_limit` in a separate migration
95: - Cover all GitLab.com plans (`default`, `free`, `premium`, `premium_trial`, `ultimate`, `ultimate_trial`, `ultimate_trial_paid_customer`, `opensource`) in limit migrations; omitting a plan causes those customers to receive the default (possibly `0`/unlimited)
96: - Use `PlanLimits#exceeded?` or the `Limitable` concern to enforce limits; DO NOT implement ad-hoc count checks
97: - Use `Rack::Attack` for middleware-level rate limiting and `Gitlab::ApplicationRateLimiter` for controller/API-level throttling
98: 
99: ### Backwards Compatibility Across Updates
100:

5: <!-- Auto-generated from docs.gitlab.com by gitlab-ai-principles-distiller — do not edit manually -->
6: 
7: # Sidekiq Workers Principles
8: 
9: ## Checklist
10: 
11: ### Worker Class Structure
12: 
13: - Include `ApplicationWorker` instead of `Sidekiq::Worker` in all workers.
14: - Set `data_consistency` explicitly on every worker (RuboCop enforces this).
15: - Define a known `feature_category` on every Sidekiq worker.
16: - Mark workers as `idempotent!` unless there is a documented reason they cannot be.
17: - Use `deduplicate :until_executing` (default) or `:until_executed` alongside `idempotent!`; pass `including_scheduled: true` if future-scheduled jobs should also be deduplicated.
18: - DO NOT mark a worker as both `urgency :high` and `worker_has_external_dependencies!`.
19: - DO NOT mark a worker as both `urgency :high` and `worker_resource_boundary :memory`.
20: - Prepend `::Geo::SkipSecondary` to workers that attempt database writes if they can be enqueued on Geo secondary sites.
21: - Scope all Sidekiq jobs to a single organization; allow cross-organization jobs only when the job is both a recurring cron job and idempotent.
22: 
23: ### Sharding
24: 
25: - Wrap all Sidekiq API calls in `Sidekiq::Client.via(pool)` using `Gitlab::SidekiqSharding::Router.get_shard_instance`.
26: - Use `Gitlab::SidekiqSharding::Validator.allow_unrouted_sidekiq_calls` only for components that do not affect GitLab.com; add a comment explaining why it is safe.
27: 
28: ### Retries and Failure Handling
29: 
30: - Use `find_by_id` (not `find`) for database lookups in `perform`, and return early if the record is nil, to guard against state changes between scheduling and execution.
31: - Prefer the default of 25 retries; only lower the retry count for workers contacting external services without delivery guarantees, non-idempotent workers, or high-frequency cron jobs.
32: - Use an exception class inheriting from `Gitlab::SidekiqMiddleware::RetryError` when retrying without tracking in Sentry.
33: - Add permanent failure handling to `sidekiq_retries_exhausted`, not inline in `perform`.
34: - Allow exceptions to propagate so Sidekiq can reschedule; DO NOT swallow exceptions silently.
35: 
36: ### Concurrency Limit
37: 
38: - Set a `concurrency_limit` for all workers to prevent system overload.
39: - Use only boolean (fully on/off) feature flags when rolling out a concurrency limit — DO NOT use percentage-based rollouts with `Feature.current_request`.
40: - Use a lambda returning `nil` or `0` to disable the limit; use a negative number to pause execution.
41: - Use `max_concurrency_limit_percentage` to override the default shard capacity percentage when the GitLab.com default is not appropriate for the worker.
42: 
43: ### Job Urgency
44: 
45: - Set `urgency :high` only for jobs where median execution time is under 1 second and 99th percentile is under 10 seconds.
46: - Ping `@gitlab-com/gl-infra/data-access/durability` on the MR when changing a queue's urgency if the expected shard load increase exceeds 5%.
47: 
48: ### Data Consistency
49: 
50: - Prefer `data_consistency :sticky` for jobs that must execute quickly.
51: - Use `data_consistency :delayed` for jobs where delayed execution is acceptable (cache expiration, webhooks); DO NOT use `:delayed` for cron jobs where retry is disabled.
52: - Use `data_consistency :always` only for jobs with documented edge cases around primary stickiness; it is strongly discouraged.
53: - Use `overrides:` keyword with `data_consistency` when a worker's database usage is skewed toward a specific decomposed database (e.g., `ci`).
54: - Use the `feature_flag:` property with `data_consistency` to safely toggle load balancing for a specific job; DO NOT use actor-based feature gates (project, group, user) — use percentage-of-time rollout only.
55: 
56: ### Job Parameters
57: 
58: - Ensure hash keys and values in worker parameters are native JSON types (strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, objects, null).
59: - Add a test using `param_containing_valid_native_json_types` matcher for code that generates worker parameters.
60: - Order `perform` parameters: highest-level resource IDs → user IDs → lower-level resource IDs → optional config hash.
61: - Place non-core or frequently-changing parameters inside a trailing hash (`params = {}`) rather than as positional arguments.
62: - DO NOT refactor existing workers' parameter order or structure (breaks queue compatibility).
63: 
64: ### Compatibility Across Updates
65: 
66: - Add new arguments to `perform` with a default value first (Release M), update call sites in Release M+1, remove the default in Release M+2.
67: - Deprecate removed arguments with a default of `nil` and a comment before removing them across two releases.
68: - Control new worker scheduling with a feature flag to avoid scheduling before Sidekiq deployment completes.
69: - When removing a worker: make `perform` a no-op in Release M, add a migration using `sidekiq_remove_jobs` in Release M+1, delete the class in Release M+2.
70: - Use `sidekiq_queue_migrate` in a **post-deployment migration** (not a standard migration) when renaming queues.
71: - When renaming a worker class, have the old worker delegate to the new worker's `perform` in Release M, enable scheduling of the new worker in Release M+1, remove the old class in Release M+2.
72: 
73: ### Queue and Namespace
74: 
75: - Run `bin/rake gitlab:sidekiq:all_queues_yml:generate` and `bin/rake gitlab:sidekiq:sidekiq_queues_yml:generate` after adding a new worker.
76: - DO NOT manually override the queue name with `sidekiq_options queue:` unless there is a specific documented reason.
77: - Use `queue_namespace :cronjob` (via `CronjobQueue`) for cron-scheduled workers.
78: 
79: ### Versioning
80: 
81: - Increment `version` on the worker class when changing `perform` arguments, and ensure the worker handles all prior argument formats.
82: 
83: ### Job Size
84: 
85: - DO NOT pass large objects as job arguments; if compressed arguments exceed 5 MB, store data in object storage or reload from the database inside the job.
86: 
87: ### Logging and Context
88: 
89: - Use `loggable_arguments` to explicitly allowlist non-numeric arguments that are safe to log; DO NOT rely on default logging for string arguments containing sensitive data.
90: - Wrap job scheduling in `with_context` or use `bulk_perform_async_with_contexts` / `bulk_perform_in_with_contexts` when scheduling from cron workers to propagate correct context.
91: - Pre-load the route for namespaces and projects (`.with_route`) before passing them to context helpers.
92: 
93: ### Deferring Workers
94: 
95: - Use `defer_on_database_health_signal` with `gitlab_schema`, `delay_by`, and `tables` parameters to opt in to automatic deferral based on database health indicators.
96: 
97: ### LimitedCapacity::Worker
98: 
99: - Implement `perform_work`, `remaining_work_count`, and `max_running_jobs` when using `LimitedCapacity::Worker`.
100: - Schedule `LimitedCapacity::Worker` subclasses via `perform_with_capacity`, not `perform_async`; always pass the same `*args` on every invocation.

<content>
1: ---
2: source_checksum: 397e954034ef7a8e
3: distilled_at_sha: 38eec71eeabc7ee15c3c39204fae8e675609f903
4: ---
5: <!-- Auto-generated from docs.gitlab.com by gitlab-ai-principles-distiller — do not edit manually -->
6: 
7: # Permissions Fundamentals Principles
8: 
9: ## Checklist
10: 
11: ### Authorization Enforcement
12: 
13: - Enforce authorization using defense-in-depth: implement permission checks at multiple layers (finders, services, GraphQL, REST API, controllers).
14: - Enforce authorization in shared business logic (services and finders) as the primary layer, so checks are consistent across REST, GraphQL, and controllers.
15: - DO NOT rely solely on display-layer permission checks (such as showing/hiding buttons) as a security control — always back them with backend enforcement.
16: - Prefer domain logic (services or finders) as the source of truth when making exceptions to multi-layer enforcement.
17: - When a class accepts `current_user`, ensure it is responsible for authorization.
18: - When adding a new API endpoint, check an existing ability or add a new one; do not skip the authorization check.
19: - When using an ability check in UI elements, ensure the underlying backend code also performs the same ability check.
20: - Use `push_frontend_ability` (available on controllers inheriting from `ApplicationController`) to expose abilities to JavaScript; access them via `gon.abilities.<camelCaseName>` (DO NOT use `gon.abilities.<snake_case_name>`).
21: 
22: ### Permission Naming Conventions
23: 
24: - Introduce a new permission only when no existing permission covers the same subject and action; reuse existing permissions wherever possible.
25: - Follow the naming pattern `action_resource(_subresource)` for all permissions (both assignable and raw).
26: - Use only the preferred actions — `create`, `read`, `update`, `delete` — unless a documented exception applies; obtain Authorization team approval for any action outside this set.
27: - DO NOT introduce disallowed actions: `admin`, `change`, `configure`, `destroy`, `edit`, `list`, `manage`, `modify`, `set`, `view`, or `write`.
28: - Use the singular form for the resource segment (e.g., `read_project`, not `read_projects`).
29: - Match the resource name to the domain object being acted upon, using user-facing terminology rather than implementation details.
30: - DO NOT encode the resource boundary (`project`, `group`, `user`) into the permission name; pass the subject in the `can?` call to determine scope instead (e.g., use `read_insights_dashboard` not `read_project_insights_dashboard`).
31: - Introduce a new action outside the preferred set only when it represents a distinct lifecycle transition (`archive_project`), a relationship change (`transfer_project`), or a high-impact/irreversible operation (`purge_maven_virtual_registry_cache`) already established in GitLab domain language.
32: 
33: ### Private Permissions
34: 
35: - Prefix private permissions with an underscore (`_`) following the pattern `_<action>_<qualifier>_<resource>` (e.g., `_read_authored_issue`).
36: - DO NOT check private permissions at enforcement points (controllers, services, finders, GraphQL); use them only inside policy files.
37: - Use private permissions when a role can perform an action only when a subject-level condition is met, or when different roles have different levels of access to the same action.
38: - DO NOT use private permissions for feature flag checks, license checks, or settings-based restrictions — use `prevent` rules instead.
39: - Create a definition file for every private permission under `config/authz/permissions/<resource>/_<action>_<qualifier>.yml`.
40: - Use qualifiers that correspond to a real attribute or relationship on the resource (e.g., `confidential`, `authored`, `assigned`); DO NOT use qualifiers that describe the actor.
41: - Prefer past-participle or adjective forms for qualifiers that read naturally as a description of the resource; introduce qualifiers only when the same action on the same resource requires different access levels depending on the resource's state.
42: 
43: ### Role Definition YAML Files
44: 
45: - Define default roles as YAML files under `config/authz/roles/` with the fields `name`, `description`, `inherits_from`, and optionally `raw_permissions` and `permissions`.
46: - Understand that the final permission set for a role is the union of its `raw_permissions`, expanded `permissions` (assignable groups), and all permissions inherited recursively from parent roles.
47: - When adding a permission to a role, prefer using an assignable permission group; use `raw_permissions` only for existing permissions that need to be moved into the role definition.
48: - When removing a permission from a role, verify it is not depended on by other features; use `GITLAB_DEBUG_POLICIES=true` to trace where a permission is checked.
49: 
50: ### Custom Roles
51: 
52: - Run `./ee/bin/custom-ability <ABILITY_NAME>` to generate the YAML configuration file for a new custom ability under `ee/config/custom_abilities/`.
53: - Run `bundle exec rails generate gitlab:custom_roles:code --ability <ABILITY_NAME>` to update the permissions validation schema and create a spec file.
54: - Define permissions declaratively in the custom ability YAML using `project_permissions` and `group_permissions` fields; DO NOT manually add policy rules to `ProjectPolicy` or `GroupPolicy` for custom abilities.
55: - Mark a custom ability as `wip: true` in its YAML when implementing across multiple MRs; remove the `wip:` key when the ability is ready to ship.
56: - Consolidate custom role abilities to a minimum: use `read_*` for all view-related actions and `admin_*` for object updates; avoid introducing additional abilities unless necessary.
57: - Ensure `admin_*` custom abilities declare `read_*` as a requirement in the YAML `requirements` field.
58: - Add the ability as a trait in the `MemberRoles` factory (`ee/spec/factories/member_roles.rb`) and add request specs under `ee/spec/requests/custom_roles/<ABILITY_NAME>/request_spec.rb`.
59: - Run `bundle exec rake gitlab:custom_roles:compile_docs` and `bundle exec rake gitlab:graphql:compile_docs` to update documentation after adding a custom ability.
60: - Assess privilege escalation risk when adding new custom permissions that interact with GitLab artifacts (such as access tokens) that encode the base role.
61: 
62: ### Internal Permission Groups
63: 
64: - Define internal permission groups as YAML files under `config/authz/permission_groups/internal/<resource>/<name>.yml` with `description` and `permissions` fields.
65: - Use internal permission groups in `DeclarativePolicy` policies by calling `Authz::PermissionGroups::Internal.get('<identifier>')` and splatting the result into a `prevent` rule.
66: - Use internal permission groups when a resource state change (archiving, locking) should disable many permissions at once, or when the same permission set is referenced from multiple places.
67: - DO NOT use internal permission groups for role-based permission assignment (use role definitions or custom roles), or for token-scoped permissions (use assignable permissions).
68: 
69: ### Predefined Roles and Visibility
70: 
71: - Store project membership (with group membership already resolved) in the `project_authorizations` table; the highest permission across group and project membership is the effective access level.
72: - DO NOT grant subgroups a higher visibility level than their parent group.
73: - Design complex feature permissions as separate, granular permissions with explicit dependency relationships rather than reusing a single broad permission.
74: 
75: ### Policy Testing
76: 
77: - Place policy unit tests in `spec/policies/` (CE) or `ee/spec/policies/` (EE).
78: - Use one `describe` block per permission; DO NOT group multiple permissions into a single block.
79: - Use `where` table syntax to test each role explicitly, and include every role in the table — DO NOT omit roles expected to be disallowed.
80: - Define the subject explicitly inside each `describe` block using `let_it_be`; DO NOT rely on a subject defined at a higher scope.
81: - Use descriptively named fixtures that reflect the visibility or state under test (e.g., `private_project`, `archived_project`) rather than a generic `project`.
82: - Test both feature flag disabled and licensed feature disabled contexts for every permission that is gated by either.
83: 
84: ## Authoritative sources
85: 
86: For the full picture, see:
87: 
88: - doc/development/permissions/authorizations.md
89: - doc/development/permissions/conventions.md
90: - doc/development/permissions/role_definitions.md
91: - doc/development/permissions/predefined_roles.md
92: - doc/development/permissions/custom_roles.md
93: - doc/development/permissions/internal_permission_groups.md
94: - doc/development/permissions/testing_guidelines.md

1093:       # Although there is a race between setting rebase_jid here and clearing it

1096:       # for a completed RebaseWorker is equivalent to a nil JID.
1097:       jid = Sidekiq::Worker.skipping_transaction_check do
1098:         RebaseWorker.with_status.perform_async(id, user_id, skip_ci)
1099:       end
1100:

1102:     end

1824:   def cache_closing_issues_after_merge!(current_user)
1825:     return unless merge_commit || squash_commit
1826: 
1827:     messages = [squash_commit&.safe_message, merge_commit&.safe_message].compact
1828: 
1829:     squash_and_merge_commit_issue_ids = Gitlab::ClosingIssueExtractor.new(project, current_user)
1830:       .closed_by_message(messages.join("\n"))
1831:       .reject { |issue| issue.is_a?(ExternalIssue) }
1832:       .map(&:id)
1833: 
1834:     transaction do
1835:       update_cached_closing_issues_from_description!(squash_and_merge_commit_issue_ids)
1836:       existing_issue_ids = merge_request_closing_issues.pluck(:issue_id)
1837:       issue_ids_to_create = squash_and_merge_commit_issue_ids - existing_issue_ids
1838: 
1839:       bulk_insert_cached_closing_issues(issue_ids_to_create)
1840:     end
1841:   end

1843:   # If the merge request closes any issues, save this information in the
1844:   # `MergeRequestsClosingIssues` model. This is a performance optimization.
1845:   # Calculating this information for a number of merge requests requires
1846:   # running `ReferenceExtractor` on each of them separately.
1847:   # This optimization does not apply to issues from external sources.
1848:   def cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user = self.author)
1849:     return if closed? || merged?
1850: 
1851:     issues_to_close_ids = closes_issues(current_user).reject { |issue| issue.is_a?(ExternalIssue) }.map(&:id)
1852: 
1853:     transaction do
1854:       merge_request_closing_issues.from_mr_description.delete_all
1855: 
1856:       updated_issue_ids = update_cached_closing_issues_from_description!(issues_to_close_ids)
1857:       issue_ids_to_create = issues_to_close_ids - updated_issue_ids
1858:       next unless issue_ids_to_create.any?
1859: 
1860:       bulk_insert_cached_closing_issues(issue_ids_to_create)
1861:     end
1862:   end

1867:                          closes_issues(current_user)
1868:                        else
1869:                          Ability.issues_readable_by_user(cached_closes_issues, current_user)
1870:                        end
1871: 
1872:       ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(

1874:         associations: :project

1877:       visible_issues.select do |issue|

2430:     # It is not guaranteed that Note#created_at will be strictly later than
2431:     # MergeRequestMetric#merged_at. Nanoseconds on MySQL may break this
2432:     # comparison, as will a HA environment if clocks are not *precisely*
2433:     # synchronized. Add a minute's leeway to compensate for both possibilities
2434:     cutoff = merged_at - 1.minute
2435: 
2436:     notes_association = notes_with_associations.where('created_at >= ?', cutoff)
2437: 
2438:     !commit.has_been_reverted?(current_user, notes_association)

2463:     return if new_diff_refs == old_diff_refs
2464: 
2465:     active_diff_discussions = self.notes.new_diff_notes.discussions.select do |discussion|
2466:       discussion.active?(old_diff_refs)

2468:     return if active_diff_discussions.empty?
2469: 
2470:     paths = active_diff_discussions.flat_map { |n| n.diff_file.paths }.uniq
2471: 
2472:     active_discussions_resolved = active_diff_discussions.all?(&:resolved?)
2473: 
2474:     service = Discussions::UpdateDiffPositionService.new(
2475:       self.project,

2477:       old_diff_refs: old_diff_refs,
2478:       new_diff_refs: new_diff_refs,
2479:       paths: paths
2480:     )
2481: 
2482:     active_diff_discussions.each do |discussion|
2483:       service.execute(discussion)
2484:       discussion.clear_memoized_values
2485:     end
2486: 
2487:     # If they were all already resolved, this method will have already been called.
2488:     # If they all don't get resolved, we don't need to call the method
2489:     # If they go from unresolved -> resolved, then we call the method
2490:     if !active_discussions_resolved &&
2491:         active_diff_discussions.all?(&:resolved?) &&
2492:         project.resolve_outdated_diff_discussions?
2493:       MergeRequests::ResolvedDiscussionNotificationService
2494:         .new(project: project, current_user: current_user)
2495:         .execute(self)

3146:   def with_rebase_lock
3147:     with_retried_nowait_lock { yield }
3148:   end
3149: 
3150:   # If the merge request is idle in transaction or has a SELECT FOR
3151:   # UPDATE, we don't want to block indefinitely or this could cause a
3152:   # queue of SELECT FOR UPDATE calls. Instead, try to get the lock for
3153:   # 5 s before raising an error to the user.
3154:   def with_retried_nowait_lock
3155:     # Try at most 0.25 + (1.5 * .25) + (1.5^2 * .25) ... (1.5^5 * .25) = 5.2 s to get the lock
3156:     Retriable.retriable(on: ActiveRecord::LockWaitTimeout, tries: 6, base_interval: 0.25) do
3157:       with_lock('FOR UPDATE NOWAIT') do
3158:         yield
3159:       end
3160:     end
3161:   rescue ActiveRecord::LockWaitTimeout => e
3162:     Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_exception(e)
3163:     raise RebaseLockTimeout, _('Failed to enqueue the rebase operation, possibly due to a long-lived transaction. Try again later.')
3164:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Issuable concern
4: #
5: # Contains common functionality shared between Issues and MergeRequests
6: #
7: # Used by Issue, MergeRequest, Epic
8: #
9: module Issuable
10:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
11:   include Gitlab::SQL::Pattern
12:   include Redactable
13:   include CacheMarkdownField
14:   include Participable
15:   include Mentionable
16:   include Milestoneable
17:   include Subscribable
18:   include StripAttribute
19:   include Awardable
20:   include Taskable
21:   include Importable
22:   include Transitionable
23:   include Editable
24:   include AfterCommitQueue
25:   include Sortable
26:   include CreatedAtFilterable
27:   include UpdatedAtFilterable
28:   include ClosedAtFilterable
29:   include VersionedDescription
30:   include SortableTitle
31:   include Exportable
32:   include ReportableChanges
33:   include Import::HasImportSource
34: 
35:   TITLE_LENGTH_MAX = 255
36:   SEARCHABLE_FIELDS = %w[title description].freeze
37:   MAX_NUMBER_OF_ASSIGNEES_OR_REVIEWERS = 200
38: 
39:   STATE_ID_MAP = {
40:     opened: 1,
41:     closed: 2,
42:     merged: 3,
43:     locked: 4
44:   }.with_indifferent_access.freeze
45: 
46:   included do
47:     cache_markdown_field :title, pipeline: :single_line
48:     cache_markdown_field :description, issuable_reference_expansion_enabled: true
49: 
50:     redact_field :description
51: 
52:     belongs_to :author, class_name: 'User'
53:     belongs_to :updated_by, class_name: 'User'
54:     belongs_to :last_edited_by, class_name: 'User'
55: 
56:     has_many :notes, -> { extending Notes::ParticipantAssociationsExtension }, as: :noteable, inverse_of: :noteable, dependent: :destroy # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
57:     has_many :notes_with_possible_mentions, -> {
58:       Note.from_union(
59:         [
60:           user,
61:           system.with_possible_mentions
62:         ], remove_duplicates: false
63:       ).extending(Notes::ParticipantAssociationsExtension)
64:     }, class_name: 'Note', as: :noteable, inverse_of: :noteable
65: 
66:     has_many :note_authors, -> { distinct }, through: :notes, source: :author
67:     has_many :user_note_authors, -> { distinct.where("notes.system = false") }, through: :notes, source: :author
68: 
69:     has_many :label_links, as: :target, inverse_of: :target
70:     has_many :labels, through: :label_links
71:     has_many :todos, as: :target
72: 
73:     delegate :name,
74:       :email,
75:       :public_email,
76:       to: :author,
77:       allow_nil: true,
78:       prefix: true
79: 
80:     validates :author, presence: true
81:     validates :title, presence: true, length: { maximum: TITLE_LENGTH_MAX }
82:     # we validate the description against the limit only for Issuables being created and on updates if
83:     # the description changes to avoid breaking the existing Issuables which may have their descriptions longer
84:     validates :description, bytesize: { maximum: -> { Gitlab::CurrentSettings.description_and_note_max_size } },
85:       if: :validate_description_length?
86:     validate :validate_assignee_size_length, unless: :importing?
87: 
88:     before_validation :truncate_description_on_import!
89: 
90:     scope :authored, ->(user) { where(author_id: user) }
91:     scope :not_authored, ->(user) { where.not(author_id: user) }
92:     scope :recent, -> { reorder(id: :desc) }
93:     scope :of_projects, ->(ids) { where(project_id: ids) }
94:     scope :with_state, ->(*states) { where(state_id: states.flatten.map { |s| STATE_ID_MAP[s] }) }
95:     scope :opened, -> { with_state(:opened) }
96:     scope :closed, -> { with_state(:closed) }
97: 
98:     # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/SqlInjection
99:     # The `assignee_association_name` method is not an user input.
100:     scope :assigned, -> do
101:       where("EXISTS (SELECT TRUE FROM #{assignee_association_name}_assignees \
102:         WHERE #{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id)")
103:     end
104:     scope :unassigned, -> do
105:       where("NOT EXISTS (SELECT TRUE FROM #{assignee_association_name}_assignees \
106:         WHERE #{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id)")
107:     end
108:     scope :assigned_to, ->(users) do
109:       assignees_class = reflect_on_association("#{assignee_association_name}_assignees").klass
110: 
111:       condition = assignees_class.where(user_id: users)
112:         .where(Arel.sql("#{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id"))
113:       where(condition.arel.exists)
114:     end
115:     scope :not_assigned_to, ->(users) do
116:       assignees_class = reflect_on_association("#{assignee_association_name}_assignees").klass
117: 
118:       condition = assignees_class.where(user_id: users)
119:         .where(Arel.sql("#{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id"))
120:       where(condition.arel.exists.not)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Noteable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   # This object is used to gather noteable meta data for list displays
7:   # avoiding n+1 queries and improving performance.
8:   NoteableMeta = Struct.new(:user_notes_count)
9: 
10:   MAX_NOTES_LIMIT = 5_000
11: 
12:   class_methods do
13:     # `Noteable` class names that support replying to individual notes.
14:     def replyable_types
15:       %w[Issue MergeRequest AbuseReport WikiPage::Meta]
16:     end
17: 
18:     # `Noteable` class names that support resolvable notes.
19:     def resolvable_types
20:       %w[Issue MergeRequest DesignManagement::Design AbuseReport WikiPage::Meta]
21:     end
22: 
23:     # `Noteable` class names that support creating/forwarding individual notes.
24:     def email_creatable_types
25:       %w[Issue]
26:     end
27:   end
28: 
29:   # The timestamp of the note (e.g. the :created_at or :updated_at attribute if provided via
30:   # API call)
31:   def system_note_timestamp
32:     @system_note_timestamp || Time.current # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
33:   end
34: 
35:   attr_writer :system_note_timestamp
36: 
37:   def base_class_name
38:     self.class.base_class.name
39:   end
40: 
41:   # Convert this Noteable class name to a format usable by notifications.
42:   #
43:   # Examples:
44:   #
45:   #   noteable.class           # => MergeRequest
46:   #   noteable.human_class_name # => "merge request"
47:   def human_class_name
48:     @human_class_name ||= base_class_name.titleize.downcase
49:   end
50: 
51:   def supports_resolvable_notes?
52:     self.class.resolvable_types.include?(base_class_name)
53:   end
54: 
55:   def supports_discussions?
56:     DiscussionNote.noteable_types.include?(base_class_name)
57:   end
58: 
59:   def supports_replying_to_individual_notes?
60:     supports_discussions? && self.class.replyable_types.include?(base_class_name)
61:   end
62: 
63:   def supports_replying_to_system_notes?
64:     false
65:   end
66: 
67:   def supports_creating_notes_by_email?
68:     self.class.email_creatable_types.include?(base_class_name)
69:   end
70: 
71:   def supports_suggestion?
72:     false
73:   end
74: 
75:   def discussions_rendered_on_frontend?
76:     false
77:   end
78: 
79:   def preloads_discussion_diff_highlighting?
80:     false
81:   end
82: 
83:   def has_any_diff_note_positions?
84:     notes.any? && DiffNotePosition.where(note: notes).exists?
85:   end
86: 
87:   def discussion_notes
88:     notes
89:   end
90: 
91:   delegate :find_discussion, to: :discussion_notes
92: 
93:   def discussions
94:     @discussions ||= discussion_notes
95:       .inc_relations_for_view(self)
96:       .discussions(self)
97:   end
98: 
99:   def discussion_ids_relation
100:     notes.select(:discussion_id)
101:       .group(:discussion_id)
102:       .order('MIN(created_at), MIN(id)')
103:   end
104: 
105:   # This does not consider OutOfContextDiscussions in MRs
106:   # where notes from commits are overridden so that they have
107:   # the same discussion_id
108:   def discussion_root_note_ids(notes_filter:, sort: :created_asc)
109:     sort = :created_asc if sort.nil?
110:     relations = []
111: 
112:     relations << discussion_notes.select(
113:       "'notes' AS table_name",
114:       'MIN(id) AS id',
115:       'MIN(created_at) AS created_at',
116:       'ARRAY_AGG(id) AS ids'
117:     ).with_notes_filter(notes_filter)
118:      .group(:discussion_id)
119: 
120:     if notes_filter != UserPreference::NOTES_FILTERS[:only_comments]

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Referable concern
4: #
5: # Contains functionality related to making a model referable in Markdown, such
6: # as "#1", "!2", "~3", etc.
7: module Referable
8:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
9: 
10:   # Returns the String necessary to reference this object in Markdown
11:   #
12:   # from - Referring parent object
13:   #
14:   # This should be overridden by the including class.
15:   #
16:   # Examples:
17:   #
18:   #   Issue.first.to_reference               # => "#1"
19:   #   Issue.last.to_reference(other_project) # => "cross-project#1"
20:   #
21:   # Returns a String
22:   def to_reference(_from = nil, full:)
23:     ''
24:   end
25: 
26:   # If this referable object can serve as the base for the
27:   # reference of child objects (e.g. projects are the base of
28:   # issues), but it is formatted differently, then you may wish
29:   # to override this method.
30:   def to_reference_base(from = nil, full:, absolute_path: false)
31:     to_reference(from, full: full)
32:   end
33: 
34:   def reference_link_text(from = nil)
35:     to_reference(from)
36:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Approvable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5:   include FromUnion
6: 
7:   included do
8:     has_many :approvals, dependent: :delete_all # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
9:     has_many :approved_by_users, through: :approvals, source: :user
10: 
11:     scope :without_approvals, -> { left_outer_joins(:approvals).where(approvals: { id: nil }) }
12:     scope :with_approvals, -> { joins(:approvals) }
13:     scope :approved_by_users_with_ids, ->(*user_ids) do
14:       with_approvals
15:         .merge(Approval.with_user)
16:         .where(users: { id: user_ids })
17:         .allow_cross_joins_across_databases(url: 'https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/422085')
18:         .group(:id)
19:         .having("COUNT(users.id) = ?", user_ids.size)
20:     end
21:     scope :approved_by_users_with_usernames, ->(*usernames) do
22:       with_approvals
23:         .merge(Approval.with_user)
24:         .where(users: { username: usernames })
25:         .allow_cross_joins_across_databases(url: 'https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/422085')
26:         .group(:id)
27:         .having("COUNT(users.id) = ?", usernames.size)
28:     end
29: 
30:     scope :not_approved_by_users_with_usernames, ->(usernames) do
31:       users = User.where(username: usernames).select(:id)
32:       app_table = Approval.arel_table
33: 
34:       where(
35:         Approval.where(approvals: { user_id: users })
36:         .where(app_table[:merge_request_id].eq(arel_table[:id]))
37:         .select('true')
38:         .arel.exists.not
39:       ).allow_cross_joins_across_databases(url: 'https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/422085')
40:     end
41: 
42:     scope :with_existing_approval, -> do
43:       where_exists(Approval.where(arel_table[:id].eq(Approval.arel_table[:merge_request_id])))
44:     end
45:   end
46: 
47:   class_methods do
48:     def select_from_union(relations)
49:       where(id: from_union(relations))
50:     end
51:   end
52: 
53:   def approved_by?(user)
54:     return false unless user
55: 
56:     approvals.where(user: user).any?
57:   end
58: 
59:   def approved?
60:     approvals.present?
61:   end
62: 
63:   def eligible_for_approval_by?(user)
64:     user.present? && !approved_by?(user) && user.can?(:approve_merge_request, self)
65:   end
66: 
67:   def eligible_for_unapproval_by?(user)
68:     user && approved_by?(user) && user.can?(:approve_merge_request, self)
69:   end
70: 
71:   def approvals_for_user_ids(user_ids)
72:     approvals.where(user_id: user_ids)
73:   end
74: 
75:   # Overridden in EE::VisibleApprovable to invalidate the mergeability approval cache.
76:   # No-op in CE since the mergeability approval cache only exists in EE.
77:   def delete_approval_mergeability_cache; end
78: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class CreateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     def execute
6:       set_projects!
7:       set_default_attributes!
8: 
9:       merge_request = MergeRequest.new
10:       merge_request.target_project = @project
11:       merge_request.source_project = @source_project
12:       merge_request.source_branch = params[:source_branch]
13: 
14:       merge_after = params.delete(:merge_after)
15: 
16:       created_merge_request = create(merge_request)
17: 
18:       UpdateMergeScheduleService.new(created_merge_request, merge_after: merge_after).execute
19: 
20:       created_merge_request
21:     end
22: 
23:     def after_create(issuable)
24:       invalidate_all_users_cache_count(issuable)
25: 
26:       current_user_id = current_user.id
27: 
28:       issuable.run_after_commit do
29:         # Add new items to MergeRequests::AfterCreateService if they can
30:         # be performed in Sidekiq
31:         NewMergeRequestWorker.perform_async(issuable.id, current_user_id)
32:       end
33: 
34:       issuable.mark_as_preparing
35: 
36:       super
37:     end
38: 
39:     # expose issuable create method so it can be called from email
40:     # handler CreateMergeRequestHandler
41:     public :create
42: 
43:     private
44: 
45:     def before_create(merge_request)
46:       # If the fetching of the source branch occurs in an ActiveRecord
47:       # callback (e.g. after_create), a database transaction will be
48:       # open while the Gitaly RPC waits. To avoid an idle in transaction
49:       # timeout, we do this before we attempt to save the merge request.
50: 
51:       merge_request.source_branch_exists?
52:       merge_request.target_branch_exists?
53: 
54:       merge_request.skip_ensure_merge_request_diff = true
55:       merge_request.check_for_spam(user: current_user, action: :create)
56:     end
57: 
58:     def set_projects!
59:       # @project is used to determine whether the user can set the merge request's
60:       # assignee, milestone and labels. Whether they can depends on their
61:       # permissions on the target project.
62:       @source_project = @project
63:       @project = Project.find(params[:target_project_id]) if params[:target_project_id]
64: 
65:       # make sure that source/target project ids are not in
66:       # params so it can't be overridden later when updating attributes
67:       # from params when applying quick actions
68:       params.delete(:source_project_id)
69:       params.delete(:target_project_id)
70: 
71:       unless can?(current_user, :create_merge_request_from, @source_project) &&
72:           can?(current_user, :create_merge_request_in, @project)
73: 
74:         raise Gitlab::Access::AccessDeniedError
75:       end
76:     end
77: 
78:     def set_default_attributes!
79:       set_default_squash! unless params.key?(:squash) || params.key?('squash')
80:       set_default_force_remove_source_branch! if params[:force_remove_source_branch].nil?
81:     end
82: 
83:     def set_default_force_remove_source_branch!
84:       params[:force_remove_source_branch] = @source_project.remove_source_branch_after_merge?
85:     end
86: 
87:     def set_default_squash!
88:       params[:squash] = @project.squash_enabled_by_default?
89:     end
90:   end
91: end
92: 
93: MergeRequests::CreateService.prepend_mod_with('MergeRequests::CreateService')

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class UpdateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     include Gitlab::InternalEventsTracking
6:     extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
7: 
8:     def initialize(project:, current_user: nil, params: {})
9:       super
10: 
11:       @target_branch_was_deleted = @params.delete(:target_branch_was_deleted)
12:     end
13: 
14:     def execute(merge_request)
15:       if Gitlab::Utils.to_boolean(params[:draft])
16:         merge_request.title = merge_request.draft_title
17:       end
18: 
19:       if params.key?(:merge_after)
20:         merge_after = params.delete(:merge_after)
21:         UpdateMergeScheduleService.new(merge_request, merge_after: merge_after).execute
22:       end
23: 
24:       update_merge_request_with_specialized_service(merge_request) || general_fallback(merge_request)
25:     end
26: 
27:     def handle_changes(merge_request, options)
28:       super
29:       old_associations = options.fetch(:old_associations, {})
30:       old_labels = old_associations.fetch(:labels, [])
31:       old_mentioned_users = old_associations.fetch(:mentioned_users, [])
32:       old_assignees = old_associations.fetch(:assignees, [])
33:       old_reviewers = old_associations.fetch(:reviewers, [])
34:       old_timelogs = old_associations.fetch(:timelogs, [])
35:       changed_fields = merge_request.previous_changes.keys
36: 
37:       resolve_todos(merge_request, old_labels, old_assignees, old_reviewers)
38: 
39:       if merge_request.previous_changes.include?('title') ||
40:           merge_request.previous_changes.include?('description')
41:         todo_service.update_merge_request(merge_request, current_user, old_mentioned_users)
42:         handle_title_and_desc_edits(merge_request, merge_request.previous_changes.keys)
43:       end
44: 
45:       handle_target_branch_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
46:       handle_draft_status_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
47: 
48:       track_title_and_desc_edits(changed_fields)
49:       track_discussion_lock_toggle(merge_request, changed_fields)
50:       track_time_estimate_and_spend_edits(merge_request, old_timelogs, changed_fields)
51:       track_labels_change(merge_request, old_labels)
52: 
53:       notify_if_labels_added(merge_request, old_labels)
54:       notify_if_mentions_added(merge_request, old_mentioned_users)
55: 
56:       # Since #mark_as_unchecked triggers an update action through the MR's
57:       #   state machine, we want to push this as far down in the process so we
58:       #   avoid resetting #ActiveModel::Dirty
59:       #
60:       if changed_fields.include?('target_branch') ||
61:           changed_fields.include?('source_branch')
62:         merge_request.mark_as_unchecked unless merge_request.unchecked?
63: 
64:         # Delete HEAD diff and re-enqueue mergeability check so we get to generate
65:         # a fresh HEAD diff when either branches have been changed.
66:         merge_request.merge_head_diff&.destroy!
67:         merge_request.check_mergeability(async: true)
68:       end
69:     end
70: 
71:     def handle_task_changes(merge_request)
72:       todo_service.resolve_todos_for_target(merge_request, current_user)
73:       todo_service.update_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
74:     end
75: 
76:     def after_update(merge_request, old_associations)
77:       super
78: 
79:       merge_request.cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user) unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
80:       @trigger_work_item_updated = true
81:     end
82: 
83:     private
84: 
85:     attr_reader :target_branch_was_deleted
86: 
87:     def trigger_updated_work_item_on_closing_issues(merge_request, old_closing_issues_ids)
88:       new_issue_ids = merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.limit(1000).pluck(:issue_id) # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
89:       all_issue_ids = new_issue_ids | old_closing_issues_ids
90:       return if all_issue_ids.blank?
91: 
92:       WorkItem.id_in(all_issue_ids).find_each(batch_size: 100) do |work_item| # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
93:         GraphqlTriggers.work_item_updated(work_item)
94:       end
95:     end
96: 
97:     override :associations_before_update
98:     def associations_before_update(merge_request)
99:       super.merge(
100:         closing_issues_ids: merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.limit(1000).pluck(:issue_id) # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
101:       )
102:     end
103: 
104:     override :change_state
105:     def change_state(merge_request, state_event)
106:       return unless super
107: 
108:       @trigger_work_item_updated = true
109:     end
110: 
111:     override :trigger_update_subscriptions
112:     def trigger_update_subscriptions(merge_request, old_associations)
113:       return unless @trigger_work_item_updated
114: 
115:       trigger_updated_work_item_on_closing_issues(
116:         merge_request,
117:         old_associations.fetch(:closing_issues_ids, [])
118:       )
119:     end
120: 
121:     def general_fallback(merge_request)
122:       # We don't allow change of source/target projects and source branch
123:       # after merge request was created
124:       params.delete(:source_project_id)
125:       params.delete(:target_project_id)
126:       params.delete(:source_branch)
127: 
128:       if merge_request.closed_or_merged_without_fork?
129:         params.delete(:target_branch)
130:         params.delete(:force_remove_source_branch)
131:       end
132: 
133:       update_task_event(merge_request) || update(merge_request)
134:     end
135: 
136:     def handle_title_and_desc_edits(merge_request, changed_fields)
137:       fields = %w[title description]
138: 
139:       return unless changed_fields.any? { |field| fields.include?(field) }
140:       return unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
141:       return unless should_publish_update_event?(merge_request, changed_fields)
142: 
143:       ::Gitlab::EventStore.publish(
144:         ::MergeRequests::AutoMerge::TitleDescriptionUpdateEvent.new(data: { current_user_id: current_user.id, merge_request_id: merge_request.id })
145:       )
146:     end
147: 
148:     def should_publish_update_event?(merge_request, changed_fields)
149:       changed_fields.include?('title') &&
150:         merge_request.project.merge_request_title_regex.present?
151:     end
152: 
153:     def track_title_and_desc_edits(changed_fields)
154:       tracked_fields = %w[title description]
155: 
156:       return unless changed_fields.any? { |field| tracked_fields.include?(field) }
157: 
158:       tracked_fields.each do |action|
159:         next unless changed_fields.include?(action)
160: 
161:         merge_request_activity_counter
162:           .public_send(:"track_#{action}_edit_action", user: current_user) # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/PublicSend
163:       end
164:     end
165: 
166:     def track_discussion_lock_toggle(merge_request, changed_fields)
167:       return unless changed_fields.include?('discussion_locked')
168: 
169:       if merge_request.discussion_locked
170:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_discussion_locked_action(user: current_user)
171:       else
172:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_discussion_unlocked_action(user: current_user)
173:       end
174:     end
175: 
176:     def track_time_estimate_and_spend_edits(merge_request, old_timelogs, changed_fields)
177:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_time_estimate_changed_action(user: current_user) if changed_fields.include?('time_estimate')
178:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_time_spent_changed_action(user: current_user) if old_timelogs != merge_request.timelogs
179:     end
180: 
181:     def track_labels_change(merge_request, old_labels)
182:       return if Set.new(merge_request.labels) == Set.new(old_labels)
183: 
184:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_labels_changed_action(user: current_user)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def notify_if_labels_added(merge_request, old_labels)
188:       added_labels = merge_request.labels - old_labels
189: 
190:       return unless added_labels.present?
191: 
192:       notification_service.async.relabeled_merge_request(
193:         merge_request,
194:         added_labels,
195:         current_user
196:       )
197:     end
198: 
199:     def notify_if_mentions_added(merge_request, old_mentioned_users)
200:       added_mentions = merge_request.mentioned_users(current_user) - old_mentioned_users
201: 
202:       return unless added_mentions.present?
203: 
204:       notification_service.async.new_mentions_in_merge_request(
205:         merge_request,
206:         added_mentions,
207:         current_user
208:       )
209:     end
210: 
211:     def resolve_todos(merge_request, old_labels, old_assignees, old_reviewers)
212:       return unless has_changes?(merge_request, old_labels: old_labels, old_assignees: old_assignees, old_reviewers: old_reviewers)
213: 
214:       resolve_todos_for(merge_request)
215:     end
216: 
217:     def handle_target_branch_change(merge_request, changes)
218:       return unless changes.include?('target_branch')
219: 
220:       create_branch_change_note(
221:         merge_request,
222:         'target',
223:         target_branch_was_deleted ? 'delete' : 'update',
224:         merge_request.previous_changes['target_branch'].first,
225:         merge_request.target_branch
226:       )
227: 
228:       delete_approvals_on_target_branch_change(merge_request)
229: 
230:       # `target_branch_was_deleted` is set to true when MR gets re-targeted due to
231:       # deleted target branch. In this case we don't want to create a new pipeline
232:       # on behalf of MR author.
233:       # We nullify head_pipeline_id to force that a new pipeline is explicitly
234:       # created in order to pass mergeability checks.
235:       if target_branch_was_deleted
236:         merge_request.head_pipeline_id = nil
237:         merge_request.retargeted = true
238: 
239:         track_internal_event(
240:           'retarget_merge_request_on_target_branch_merge',
241:           user: current_user,
242:           project: merge_request.target_project
243:         )
244:       else
245:         refresh_pipelines_on_merge_requests(merge_request, allow_duplicate: true)
246:       end
247: 
248:       abort_auto_merge(merge_request, 'target branch was changed')
249:     end
250: 
251:     def handle_draft_status_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
252:       return unless changed_fields.include?("title")
253: 
254:       old_title, new_title = merge_request.previous_changes["title"]
255:       old_title_draft = MergeRequest.draft?(old_title)
256:       new_title_draft = MergeRequest.draft?(new_title)
257: 
258:       if old_title_draft != new_title_draft
259:         # notify the draft status changed. Added/removed message is handled in the
260:         # email template itself, see `change_in_merge_request_draft_status_email` template.
261:         notify_draft_status_changed(merge_request)
262:         trigger_merge_request_status_updated(merge_request)
263:         publish_draft_change_event(merge_request, new_draft_status: new_title_draft)
264:       end
265: 
266:       if !old_title_draft && new_title_draft
267:         # Marked as Draft
268:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_marked_as_draft_action(user: current_user)
269:       elsif old_title_draft && !new_title_draft
270:         # Unmarked as Draft
271:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_unmarked_as_draft_action(user: current_user)
272:       end
273:     end
274: 
275:     def publish_draft_change_event(merge_request, new_draft_status:)
276:       Gitlab::EventStore.publish(
277:         MergeRequests::DraftStateChangeEvent.new(
278:           data: {
279:             current_user_id: current_user.id,
280:             merge_request_id: merge_request.id,
281:             new_draft_status: new_draft_status
282:           }
283:         )
284:       )
285:     end
286: 
287:     def notify_draft_status_changed(merge_request)
288:       notification_service.async.change_in_merge_request_draft_status(
289:         merge_request,
290:         current_user
291:       )
292:     end
293: 
294:     def create_branch_change_note(issuable, branch_type, event_type, old_branch, new_branch)
295:       SystemNoteService.change_branch(
296:         issuable, issuable.project, current_user, branch_type, event_type,
297:         old_branch, new_branch)
298:     end
299: 
300:     override :before_update

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class AfterCreateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     def execute(merge_request)
6:       merge_request.ensure_merge_request_diff
7: 
8:       prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
9:       prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
10:       mark_merge_request_as_prepared(merge_request)
11: 
12:       logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executing hooks'))
13:       execute_hooks(merge_request)
14:       logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executed hooks'))
15:     end
16: 
17:     private
18: 
19:     def prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
20:       if async_pipeline_creation?(merge_request)
21:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline async'))
22:         create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: true)
23:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline creating async'))
24:       else
25:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline'))
26:         create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: false)
27:         merge_request.update_head_pipeline
28:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline created'))
29:       end
30: 
31:       check_mergeability(merge_request)
32:     end
33: 
34:     def prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
35:       event_service.open_mr(merge_request, current_user)
36: 
37:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_create_mr_action(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)
38:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_mr_including_ci_config(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)
39: 
40:       notification_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
41:       merge_request.diffs(include_stats: false).write_cache
42:       merge_request.create_cross_references!(current_user)
43:       todo_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
44:       merge_request.cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user)
45: 
46:       Gitlab::InternalEvents.track_event(
47:         'create_merge_request',
48:         user: current_user,
49:         project: merge_request.target_project
50:       )
51: 
52:       link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
53:     end
54: 
55:     def link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
56:       LinkLfsObjectsService.new(project: merge_request.target_project).execute(merge_request)
57:     end
58: 
59:     def check_mergeability(merge_request)
60:       return unless merge_request.preparing?
61: 
62:       # Need to set to unchecked to be able to check for mergeability or else
63:       # it'll be a no-op.
64:       merge_request.mark_as_unchecked

3: module MergeRequests

5:     extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
6:     include MergeRequests::AssignsMergeParams
7:     include MergeRequests::ErrorLogger

9:

11:
