13:       super(container: project, current_user: current_user, params: params)
14:     end
15: 
16:     def create_note(merge_request, state = merge_request.state)
17:       SystemNoteService.change_status(merge_request, merge_request.target_project, current_user, state, nil)
18:     end
19: 
20:     def hook_data(merge_request, action, old_rev: nil, old_associations: {}, system: false, system_action: nil)
21:       # NOTE: actioned_at represents when the webhook action occurred.
22:       #   Setting it to Time.current to capture when the webhook was fired for now, but we can consider allowing each service
23:       #   to provide relevant timestamps to override this later if more precision is required (e.g., approved_at, merged_at, closed_at).
24:       actioned_at = Time.current
25: 
26:       hook_data = merge_request.to_hook_data(current_user, old_associations: old_associations, action: action, actioned_at: actioned_at)
27: 
28:       if old_rev && !Gitlab::Git.blank_ref?(old_rev)
29:         hook_data[:object_attributes][:oldrev] = old_rev
30:       end
31: 
32:       hook_data[:object_attributes][:system] = system
33:       hook_data[:object_attributes][:system_action] = system_action if system && system_action
34: 
35:       hook_data
36:     end
37: 
38:     def execute_hooks(merge_request, action = 'open', old_rev: nil, old_associations: {}, system: false, system_action: nil)
39:       # NOTE: Due to the async merge request diffs generation, we need to skip this for CreateService and execute it in
40:       #   AfterCreateService instead so that the webhook consumers receive the update when diffs are ready.
41:       return if merge_request.skip_ensure_merge_request_diff
42: 
43:       merge_data = Gitlab::Lazy.new { hook_data(merge_request, action, old_rev: old_rev, old_associations: old_associations, system: system, system_action: system_action) }
44:       merge_request.project.execute_hooks(merge_data, :merge_request_hooks)
45:       merge_request.project.execute_integrations(merge_data, :merge_request_hooks)
46: 
47:       execute_external_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
48:       execute_group_mention_hooks(merge_request, merge_data) if action == 'open'
49: 
50:       enqueue_jira_connect_messages_for(merge_request)
51:     end
52: 
53:     def execute_external_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
54:       # Implemented in EE
55:     end
56: 
57:     def execute_group_mention_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
58:       return unless merge_request.instance_of?(MergeRequest)
59: 
60:       args = {
61:         'mentionable_type' => 'MergeRequest',
62:         'mentionable_id' => merge_request.id,
63:         'hook_data' => merge_data,
64:         'is_confidential' => false
65:       }
66: 
67:       merge_request.run_after_commit_or_now do
68:         Integrations::GroupMentionWorker.perform_async(args)
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def handle_changes(merge_request, options)
73:       old_associations = options.fetch(:old_associations, {})
74:       old_assignees = old_associations.fetch(:assignees, [])
75:       old_reviewers = old_associations.fetch(:reviewers, [])
76: 
77:       handle_assignees_change(merge_request, old_assignees) if merge_request.assignees != old_assignees
78:       handle_reviewers_change(merge_request, old_reviewers) if merge_request.reviewers != old_reviewers
79:     end
80: 
81:     def handle_assignees_change(merge_request, old_assignees)
82:       MergeRequests::HandleAssigneesChangeService
83:         .new(project: project, current_user: current_user)
84:         .async_execute(merge_request, old_assignees)
85:     end
86: 
87:     def handle_reviewers_change(merge_request, old_reviewers)
88:       affected_reviewers = (old_reviewers + merge_request.reviewers) - (old_reviewers & merge_request.reviewers)
89:       create_reviewer_note(merge_request, old_reviewers)
90:       notification_service.async.changed_reviewer_of_merge_request(merge_request, current_user, old_reviewers)
91:       todo_service.reassigned_reviewable(merge_request, current_user, old_reviewers)
92:       invalidate_cache_counts(merge_request, users: affected_reviewers.compact)
93:       invalidate_cache_counts(merge_request, users: merge_request.assignees)
94: 
95:       new_reviewers = merge_request.reviewers - old_reviewers
96:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_users_review_requested(users: new_reviewers)
97:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_reviewers_changed_action(user: current_user)
98:       trigger_merge_request_reviewers_updated(merge_request)
99: 
100:       set_reviewers_approved(merge_request, new_reviewers) if new_reviewers.any?
101:       set_first_reviewer_assigned_at_metrics(merge_request) if new_reviewers.any?
102:       trigger_user_merge_request_updated(merge_request)
103:       publish_assigned_reviewers_event(merge_request, new_reviewers) if new_reviewers.any?
104:     end
105: 
106:     def cleanup_environments(merge_request)
107:       Environments::StopService.new(merge_request.source_project, current_user)
108:                                .execute_for_merge_request_pipeline(merge_request)
109:     end

111:     def cancel_review_app_jobs!(merge_request)
112:       environments = merge_request.environments_in_head_pipeline.in_review_folder.available
113:       environments.each { |environment| environment.cancel_deployment_jobs! }
114:     end

118:     end

122:     end

127: 
128:       "#<#{self.class} #{merge_request.to_reference(full: true)}>"

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequestPolicy < IssuablePolicy
4:   condition(:can_approve) { can_approve? }
5: 
6:   rule { locked }.policy do
7:     prevent :reopen_merge_request
8:   end
9: 
10:   # Only users who can read the merge request can comment.
11:   # Although :read_merge_request is computed in the policy context,
12:   # it would not be safe to prevent :create_note there, since
13:   # note permissions are shared, and this would apply too broadly.
14:   rule { ~can?(:read_merge_request) }.policy do
15:     prevent :create_note
16:     prevent :accept_merge_request
17:     prevent :mark_note_as_internal
18:   end
19: 
20:   rule { can_approve }.policy do
21:     enable :approve_merge_request
22:   end
23: 
24:   rule { can?(:approve_merge_request) & bot }.policy do
25:     enable :reset_merge_request_approvals
26:   end
27: 
28:   rule { ~anonymous & can?(:read_merge_request) }.policy do
29:     enable :create_todo
30:     enable :update_subscription
31:   end
32: 
33:   rule { hidden & ~admin }.policy do
34:     prevent :read_merge_request
35:     prevent :update_merge_request
36:     prevent :approve_merge_request
37:   end
38: 
39:   condition(:can_merge) { @subject.can_be_merged_by?(@user) }
40: 
41:   rule { can_merge }.policy do
42:     enable :accept_merge_request
43:   end
44: 
45:   rule { can?(:admin_merge_request) }.policy do
46:     enable :set_merge_request_metadata
47:   end
48: 
49:   private
50: 
51:   def can_approve?
52:     can?(:update_merge_request) && is_container_member?
53:   end
54: end

11: #     state: 'open', 'closed', 'merged', 'locked', or 'all'
12: #     group_id: integer
13: #     project_id: integer
14: #     milestone_title: string
15: #     release_tag: string
16: #     author_id: integer
17: #     author_username: string
18: #     assignee_id: integer
19: #     search: string
20: #     in: 'title', 'description', or a string joining them with comma
21: #     label_name: string
22: #     sort: string
23: #     non_archived: boolean
24: #     merged_without_event_source: boolean
25: #     my_reaction_emoji: string
26: #     source_branch: string
27: #     target_branch: string
28: #     created_after: datetime

32: #     review_states: 'unreviewed', 'reviewed', 'requested_changes' or 'approved'
33: #     not:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   # MergeService class
5:   #
6:   # Do git merge and in case of success
7:   # mark merge request as merged and execute all hooks and notifications
8:   # Executed when you do merge via GitLab UI
9:   #
10:   class MergeService < MergeRequests::MergeBaseService
11:     include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
12: 
13:     GENERIC_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'An error occurred while merging'
14: 
15:     delegate :merge_jid, :state, to: :@merge_request
16: 
17:     def execute(merge_request, options = {})
18:       return if merge_request.merged?
19:       return unless exclusive_lease(merge_request).try_obtain
20: 
21:       merge_strategy_class = options[:merge_strategy] || MergeRequests::MergeStrategies::FromSourceBranch
22:       @merge_strategy = merge_strategy_class.new(merge_request, current_user, merge_params: params, options: options)
23: 
24:       @merge_request = merge_request
25:       @options = options
26: 
27:       # Lease may already be held by another merge with the same source branch.
28:       # This is safe - the lease protects downstream MRs (via target_branch_pending_deletion_check!),
29:       # and that protection is already in place from the other process.
30:       obtain_branch_deletion_lease if delete_source_branch?
31:       jid = merge_jid
32: 
33:       validate!
34: 
35:       merge_request.in_locked_state do
36:         if commit
37:           after_merge
38:           success
39:         end
40:       end
41: 
42:       log_info("Merge process finished on JID #{jid} with state #{state}")
43:     rescue MergeError, MergeRequests::MergeStrategies::StrategyError => e
44:       handle_merge_error(log_message: e.message, save_message_on_model: true)
45:     ensure
46:       release_branch_deletion_lease unless merge_request.merged?
47:       exclusive_lease(merge_request).cancel
48:     end
49: 
50:     private
51: 
52:     def validate!
53:       authorization_check!
54:       error_check!
55:       target_branch_pending_deletion_check!
56:       validate_strategy!
57:       updated_check!
58:     end
59: 
60:     def authorization_check!
61:       unless @merge_request.can_be_merged_by?(current_user)
62:         raise_error('You are not allowed to merge this merge request')
63:       end
64:     end
65: 
66:     def validate_strategy!
67:       @merge_strategy.validate!
68:     end
69: 
70:     def target_branch_pending_deletion_check!
71:       lease_key = MergeRequests::DeleteSourceBranchWorker.lease_key(@merge_request.target_project_id, @merge_request.target_branch)
72:       return unless Gitlab::ExclusiveLease.get_uuid(lease_key)
73: 
74:       raise_error(_('The target branch is scheduled for deletion. Please wait for the branch to be retargeted.'))
75:     end
76: 
77:     def updated_check!
78:       unless source_matches?
79:         raise_error('Branch has been updated since the merge was requested. '\
80:                     'Please review the changes.')
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def commit
85:       log_info("Git merge started on JID #{merge_jid}")
86: 
87:       merge_result = try_merge { @merge_strategy.execute_git_merge! }
88: 
89:       commit_sha = merge_result[:commit_sha]
90:       raise_error(GENERIC_ERROR_MESSAGE) unless commit_sha
91: 
92:       log_info("Git merge finished on JID #{merge_jid} commit #{commit_sha}")
93: 
94:       new_merge_request_attributes = {
95:         merged_commit_sha: commit_sha,
96:         merge_commit_sha: merge_result[:merge_commit_sha],
97:         squash_commit_sha: merge_result[:squash_commit_sha]
98:       }.compact
99: 
100:       merge_request.update!(new_merge_request_attributes) if new_merge_request_attributes.present?
101: 
102:       commit_sha
103:     ensure
104:       merge_request.update_and_mark_in_progress_merge_commit_sha(nil)
105:       log_info("Merge request marked in progress")
106:     end
107: 
108:     def try_merge
109:       yield
110:     rescue Gitlab::Git::PreReceiveError => e
111:       raise MergeError, "Something went wrong during merge pre-receive hook. #{e.message}".strip
112:     rescue StandardError => e
113:       handle_merge_error(log_message: e.message)
114:       raise_error(GENERIC_ERROR_MESSAGE)
115:     end
116: 
117:     def after_merge
118:       # Need to store `merge_jid` in a variable since `MergeRequests::PostMergeService`
119:       # will call `MergeRequest#mark_as_merged` and will unset `merge_jid`.
120:       jid = merge_jid
121: 
122:       log_info("Post merge started on JID #{jid} with state #{state}")
123:       MergeRequests::PostMergeService.new(project: project, current_user: current_user, params: { delete_source_branch:
124:                                           delete_source_branch? }).execute(merge_request)
125:       log_info("Post merge finished on JID #{jid} with state #{state}")
126: 
127:       if delete_source_branch?
128:         MergeRequests::DeleteSourceBranchWorker.perform_async(
129:           @merge_request.id,
130:           @merge_request.source_branch_sha,
131:           branch_deletion_user.id
132:         )
133:       end
134: 
135:       merge_request_merge_param
136:     end
137: 
138:     def branch_deletion_user
139:       current_user
140:     end
141: 
142:     # Verify again that the source branch can be removed, since branch may be protected,
143:     # or the source branch may have been updated, or the user may not have permission
144:     #
145:     def delete_source_branch?
146:       params.fetch('should_remove_source_branch', @merge_request.force_remove_source_branch?) &&
147:         @merge_request.can_remove_source_branch?(branch_deletion_user)
148:     end
149: 
150:     def merge_request_merge_param
151:       if @merge_request.can_remove_source_branch?(branch_deletion_user) && !params.fetch('should_remove_source_branch', nil).nil?
152:         @merge_request.update(merge_params: @merge_request.merge_params.merge('should_remove_source_branch' => params['should_remove_source_branch']))
153:       end
154:     end
155: 
156:     def handle_merge_error(log_message:, save_message_on_model: false)
157:       log_error("MergeService ERROR: #{merge_request_info}:#{merge_status} - #{log_message}")
158:       @merge_request.update(merge_error: log_message) if save_message_on_model
159:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   # PostMergeService class
5:   #
6:   # Mark existing merge request as merged
7:   # and execute all hooks and notifications
8:   #
9:   class PostMergeService < MergeRequests::BaseService
10:     include RemovesRefs
11: 
12:     MAX_RETARGET_MERGE_REQUESTS = 4
13:     BATCH_SIZE = 100
14: 
15:     def execute(merge_request, source = nil)
16:       return if merge_request.merged?
17: 
18:       # Mark the merge request as merged, everything that happens afterwards is
19:       # executed once
20:       unless merge_request.mark_as_merged
21:         Gitlab::AppLogger.warn(
22:           message: 'Failed to mark merge request as merged',
23:           merge_request_id: merge_request.id,
24:           merge_request_iid: merge_request.iid,
25:           project_id: merge_request.project_id,
26:           delete_source_branch: params[:delete_source_branch],
27:           errors: merge_request.errors.full_messages
28:         )
29:       end
30: 
31:       create_event(merge_request)
32:       todo_service.merge_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
33: 
34:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_merge_mr_action(user: current_user)
35: 
36:       create_note(merge_request, source)
37:       close_issues(merge_request)
38:       notification_service.merge_mr(merge_request, current_user)
39:       invalidate_all_users_cache_count(merge_request)
40:       merge_request.invalidate_project_counter_caches
41:       delete_non_latest_diffs(merge_request)
42:       cancel_review_app_jobs!(merge_request)
43:       cleanup_environments(merge_request)
44:       cleanup_refs(merge_request)
45:       deactivate_pages_deployments(merge_request)
46:       cancel_auto_merges_targeting_source_branch(merge_request)
47:       trigger_user_merge_request_updated(merge_request)
48: 
49:       execute_hooks(merge_request, 'merge')
50:     end
51: 
52:     def create_note(merge_request, source)
53:       SystemNoteService.change_status(
54:         merge_request,
55:         merge_request.target_project,
56:         current_user,
57:         merge_request.state,
58:         source
59:       )
60:     end
61: 
62:     private
63: 
64:     def close_issues(merge_request)
65:       return unless merge_request.target_branch == project.default_branch
66: 
67:       # We need to do this here to include closing messages from merge and squash commit
68:       merge_request.cache_closing_issues_after_merge!(current_user)
69: 
70:       if merge_request.target_project.has_external_issue_tracker?
71:         merge_request.closes_issues(current_user).each do |issue|
72:           close_issue(issue, merge_request)
73:         end
74:       else
75:         delay = 0
76:         merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.preload_issue
77:                      .find_each(batch_size: BATCH_SIZE).with_index(1) do |closing_issue, index| # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Would require exact redefinition of the method
78:           delay += 5 if (index % 5) == 0
79:           close_issue(closing_issue.issue, merge_request, !closing_issue.from_mr_description, delay: delay)
80:         end
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def close_issue(issue, merge_request, skip_authorization = false, delay: 0)
85:       # We are intentionally only closing Issues asynchronously (excluding ExternalIssues)
86:       # as the worker only supports finding an Issue. We are also only experiencing
87:       # SQL timeouts when closing an Issue.
88:       if issue.is_a?(Issue)
89:         # Doing this check here only to save a scheduled worker. The worker will also do this policy check.
90:         return if !skip_authorization && !current_user.can?(:update_issue, issue)
91:         return unless issue.autoclose_by_merged_closing_merge_request?
92: 
93:         worker_args = [project.id,
94:           current_user.id,
95:           issue.id,
96:           merge_request.id,
97:           { skip_authorization: skip_authorization }]
98:         MergeRequests::CloseIssueWorker.perform_in(delay.minutes, *worker_args)
99:       else
100:         Issues::CloseService.new(container: project, current_user: current_user).execute(issue, commit: merge_request)
101:       end
102:     end
103: 
104:     def delete_non_latest_diffs(merge_request)
105:       DeleteNonLatestDiffsService.new(merge_request).execute
106:     end
107: 
108:     def create_merge_event(merge_request, current_user)
109:       EventCreateService.new.merge_mr(merge_request, current_user)
110:     end
111: 
112:     def create_event(merge_request)
113:       # Making sure MergeRequest::Metrics updates are in sync with
114:       # Event creation.
115:       Event.transaction do
116:         merge_event = create_merge_event(merge_request, current_user)
117:         merge_request_metrics_service(merge_request).merge(merge_event)
118:       end
119:     end
120: 
121:     def cancel_auto_merges_targeting_source_branch(merge_request)
122:       return unless params[:delete_source_branch]
123: 
124:       merge_request.source_project
125:         .merge_requests
126:         .by_target_branch(merge_request.source_branch)
127:         .with_auto_merge_enabled.each do |targetting_merge_request|
128:           if targetting_merge_request.auto_merge_strategy == ::AutoMergeService::STRATEGY_MERGE_WHEN_CHECKS_PASS
129:             abort_auto_merge_with_todo(targetting_merge_request, "target branch was merged in !#{merge_request.iid}")
130:           end
131:         end
132:     end
133:   end
134: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class CloseService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     include RemovesRefs
6: 
7:     def execute(merge_request, commit = nil, skip_reset: false)
8:       return merge_request unless authorized_to_close?(merge_request)
9: 
10:       use_primary(merge_request, skip_reset) do |merge_request|
11:         # If we close MergeRequest we want to ignore validation
12:         # so we can close broken one (Ex. fork project removed)
13:         merge_request.allow_broken = true
14: 
15:         if merge_request.close
16:           expire_unapproved_key(merge_request)
17:           create_event(merge_request)
18:           merge_request_activity_counter.track_close_mr_action(user: current_user)
19:           create_note(merge_request)
20:           notification_service.async.close_mr(merge_request, current_user)
21:           todo_service.close_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
22:           execute_hooks(merge_request, 'close')
23:           invalidate_all_users_cache_count(merge_request)
24:           merge_request.invalidate_project_counter_caches
25:           cleanup_environments(merge_request)
26:           deactivate_pages_deployments(merge_request)
27:           abort_auto_merge(merge_request, 'merge request was closed')
28:           cleanup_refs(merge_request)
29:           trigger_merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
30:         end
31: 
32:         merge_request
33:       end
34:     end
35: 
36:     private
37: 
38:     def use_primary(merge_request, skip_reset)
39:       return yield(merge_request) if skip_reset
40: 
41:       ::Gitlab::Database::LoadBalancing::SessionMap.current(merge_request.load_balancer).use_primary do
42:         yield(merge_request.reset)
43:       end
44:     end
45: 
46:     def create_event(merge_request)
47:       # Making sure MergeRequest::Metrics updates are in sync with
48:       # Event creation.
49:       Event.transaction do
50:         close_event = event_service.close_mr(merge_request, current_user)
51:         merge_request_metrics_service(merge_request).close(close_event)
52:       end
53:     end
54: 
55:     def expire_unapproved_key(merge_request)
56:       nil
57:     end
58: 
59:     def trigger_merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
60:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
61:     end
62: 
63:     def authorized_to_close?(merge_request)
64:       params[:skip_authorization].present? || can?(current_user, :update_merge_request, merge_request)
65:     end
66:   end
67: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeWorker
4:   include ApplicationWorker
5: 
6:   data_consistency :always
7: 
8:   sidekiq_options retry: 3
9: 
10:   feature_category :code_review_workflow
11:   urgency :high
12:   weight 5
13:   loggable_arguments 2
14:   idempotent!
15: 
16:   deduplicate :until_executed, including_scheduled: true
17: 
18:   def perform(merge_request_id, current_user_id, params)
19:     # MergeService enqueues downstream jobs that reset the per-thread experience
20:     # context, so the reference must be captured up front. On the GraphQL and
21:     # auto-merge paths the experience is never started, and Labkit short-circuits
22:     # resume/complete on an unstarted experience.
23:     experience = Labkit::UserExperienceSli.resume(:immediate_web_merge)
24: 
25:     Gitlab::QueryLimiting.disable!('https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/464676')
26: 
27:     begin
28:       current_user = User.find(current_user_id)
29:       merge_request = MergeRequest.find(merge_request_id)
30:     rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
31:       experience.error!('merge request or user not found').complete
32:       return
33:     end
34: 
35:     params = params.with_indifferent_access
36:     params[:check_mergeability_retry_lease] = true unless params.has_key?(:check_mergeability_retry_lease)
37: 
38:     MergeRequests::MergeService.new(project: merge_request.target_project, current_user: current_user, params: params)
39:       .execute(merge_request)
40: 
41:     experience.error!(merge_request.merge_error) unless merge_request.merged?
42:     experience.complete
43:   end
44: end
45:

2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class MergeData < ApplicationRecord
5:     include PartitionedTable
6:     include ShaAttribute
7:     include Transitionable
8: 
9:     self.table_name = 'merge_requests_merge_data'
10:     self.primary_key = 'merge_request_id'
11: 
12:     partitioned_by :merge_request_id, strategy: :int_range, partition_size: 10_000_000,
13:       sequence_name: 'merge_requests_id_seq'
14: 
15:     MERGE_STATUSES = {
16:       unchecked: 0,
17:       preparing: 1,
18:       checking: 2,
19:       can_be_merged: 3,
20:       cannot_be_merged: 4,
21:       cannot_be_merged_recheck: 5,
22:       cannot_be_merged_rechecking: 6
23:     }.freeze
24: 
25:     enum :merge_status, MERGE_STATUSES, scope: false, instance_methods: false
26: 
27:     belongs_to :merge_request, inverse_of: :merge_data
28:     belongs_to :project
29:     belongs_to :merge_user, class_name: 'User'
30: 
31:     validates :project, presence: true
32:     validates :merge_request, presence: true
33:     validates :merge_status, presence: true
34: 
35:     sha_attribute :merge_commit_sha
36:     sha_attribute :merged_commit_sha
37:     sha_attribute :merge_ref_sha
38:     sha_attribute :squash_commit_sha
39:     sha_attribute :in_progress_merge_commit_sha
40: 
41:     serialize :merge_params, type: Hash # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordSerialize -- Extraction to a new table
42: 
43:     state_machine :merge_status, initial: :unchecked do
44:       event :mark_as_preparing do
45:         transition [:unchecked, :can_be_merged] => :preparing
46:       end
47: 
48:       event :mark_as_unchecked do
49:         transition [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking] => :unchecked
50:         transition [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged_recheck
51:       end
52: 
53:       event :mark_as_checking do
54:         transition unchecked: :checking
55:         transition cannot_be_merged_recheck: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
56:       end
57: 
58:       event :mark_as_mergeable do
59:         transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :can_be_merged
60:       end
61: 
62:       event :mark_as_unmergeable do
63:         transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged
64:       end
65: 
66:       state :preparing
67:       state :unchecked
68:       state :cannot_be_merged_recheck
69:       state :checking
70:       state :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
71:       state :can_be_merged
72:       state :cannot_be_merged
73: 
74:       around_transition do |merge_data, _transition, block|
75:         Gitlab::Timeless.timeless(merge_data, &block)
76:       end
77: 
78:       # TODO: Move before_transition and after_transition callbacks from MergeRequest
79:       #   when MergeRequest no longer handles merge_status transitions:
80: 
81:       def check_state?(merge_status)
82:         [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking].include?(merge_status.to_sym)
83:       end
84:     end
85: 
86:     # Returns current merge_status except it returns `cannot_be_merged_rechecking` as `checking`
87:     # to avoid exposing unnecessary internal state
88:     def public_merge_status
89:       cannot_be_merged_rechecking? || preparing? ? 'checking' : merge_status
90:     end
91:   end
92: end

3: module Gitlab
4:   module BackgroundMigration
5:     class BackfillMergeRequestsMergeDataFromMergeRequests < BatchedMigrationJob
6:       operation_name :backfill_merge_requests_merge_data_from_merge_requests
7:       feature_category :code_review_workflow
8: 
9:       def perform
10:         each_sub_batch do |sub_batch|
11:           connection.execute(<<~SQL)
12:             INSERT INTO merge_requests_merge_data (
13:               merge_request_id,
14:               project_id,
15:               merge_user_id,
16:               merge_params,
17:               merge_error,
18:               merge_jid,
19:               merge_commit_sha,
20:               merged_commit_sha,
21:               merge_ref_sha,
22:               squash_commit_sha,
23:               in_progress_merge_commit_sha,
24:               merge_status,
25:               auto_merge_enabled,
26:               squash
27:             )
28:             #{select_clause(sub_batch)}
29:             ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
30:           SQL
31:         end
32:       end
33: 
34:       private
35: 
36:       def select_clause(sub_batch)
37:         <<~SQL
38:           SELECT
39:             mr.id AS merge_request_id,
40:             mr.target_project_id AS project_id,
41:             mr.merge_user_id,
42:             mr.merge_params,
43:             mr.merge_error,
44:             mr.merge_jid,
45:             CASE
46:               WHEN mr.merge_commit_sha IS NOT NULL AND mr.merge_commit_sha != 'f'
47:               THEN decode(mr.merge_commit_sha, 'hex')
48:             END AS merge_commit_sha,
49:             CASE
50:               WHEN mr.merged_commit_sha IS NOT NULL
51:               THEN decode(encode(mr.merged_commit_sha, 'escape'), 'hex')
52:             END AS merged_commit_sha,
53:             mr.merge_ref_sha,
54:             mr.squash_commit_sha,
55:             CASE
56:               WHEN mr.in_progress_merge_commit_sha IS NOT NULL
57:               THEN decode(mr.in_progress_merge_commit_sha, 'hex')
58:             END AS in_progress_merge_commit_sha,
59:             CASE mr.merge_status
60:               WHEN 'unchecked' THEN 0
61:               WHEN 'preparing' THEN 1
62:               WHEN 'checking' THEN 2
63:               WHEN 'can_be_merged' THEN 3
64:               WHEN 'cannot_be_merged' THEN 4
65:               WHEN 'cannot_be_merged_recheck' THEN 5
66:               WHEN 'cannot_be_merged_rechecking' THEN 6
67:               ELSE 0
68:             END AS merge_status,
69:             mr.merge_when_pipeline_succeeds AS auto_merge_enabled,
70:             mr.squash
71:           FROM merge_requests AS mr
72:           LEFT JOIN merge_requests_merge_data mmd
73:             ON mmd.merge_request_id = mr.id
74:           WHERE mr.id BETWEEN #{sub_batch.first.id} AND #{sub_batch.last.id}
75:             AND mmd.merge_request_id IS NULL

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class RefreshService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
6: 
7:     attr_reader :push
8: 
9:     # Staggered delays for auto-merge worker enqueue.
10:     # Each MR gets a delay of index * INTERVAL seconds, spaced 3 seconds apart
11:     # (0s, 3s, 6s, ..., 57s) to avoid workers from the same or overlapping push
12:     # events landing at the same time. Capped at 20 MRs per push event; remaining
13:     # MRs are picked up in subsequent rounds as merges trigger new pushes.
14:     AUTO_MERGE_DELAY_INTERVAL = 3
15:     AUTO_MERGE_BATCH_LIMIT = 20
16: 
17:     def execute(oldrev, newrev, ref)
18:       @push = Gitlab::Git::Push.new(@project, oldrev, newrev, ref)
19:       return true unless @push.branch_push?
20: 
21:       refresh_merge_requests!
22:     end
23: 
24:     private
25: 
26:     def refresh_merge_requests!
27:       # n + 1: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-foss/issues/60289
28:       Gitlab::GitalyClient.allow_n_plus_1_calls { find_new_commits }
29:       # Be sure to close outstanding MRs before reloading them to avoid generating an
30:       # empty diff during a manual merge
31:       close_upon_missing_source_branch_ref
32: 
33:       post_merge_manually_merged
34: 
35:       link_forks_lfs_objects
36: 
37:       reload_merge_requests
38: 
39:       merge_requests_for_source_branch.each do |mr|
40:         outdate_suggestions(mr)
41:         abort_auto_merges(mr)
42:         mark_pending_todos_done(mr)
43:       end
44: 
45:       abort_ff_merge_requests_with_auto_merges
46: 
47:       cache_merge_requests_closing_issues
48: 
49:       merge_requests_for_source_branch.each do |mr|
50:         # Leave a system note if a branch was deleted/added
51:         comment_mr_branch_presence_changed(mr) if branch_added_or_removed?
52: 
53:         notify_about_push(mr)
54: 
55:         mark_mr_as_draft_from_commits(mr)
56:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_mr_including_ci_config(user: mr.author, merge_request: mr)
57:       end
58: 
59:       execute_async_workers
60: 
61:       true
62:     end
63: 
64:     def branch_added_or_removed?
65:       strong_memoize(:branch_added_or_removed) do
66:         @push.branch_added? || @push.branch_removed?
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     def close_upon_missing_source_branch_ref
71:       # MergeRequest#reload_diff ignores not opened MRs. This means it won't
72:       # create an `empty` diff for `closed` MRs without a source branch, keeping
73:       # the latest diff state as the last _valid_ one.
74:       merge_requests_for_source_branch.reject(&:source_branch_exists?).each do |mr|
75:         Gitlab::AppLogger.info(
76:           message: 'Closing merge request due to missing source branch',
77:           merge_request_id: mr.id,
78:           merge_request_iid: mr.iid,
79:           project_id: mr.project_id,
80:           source_branch: mr.source_branch
81:         )
82: 
83:         MergeRequests::CloseService
84:           .new(project: mr.target_project, current_user: @current_user)
85:           .execute(mr)
86:       end
87:     end
88: 
89:     # Collect open merge requests that target same branch we push into
90:     # and close if push to master include last commit from merge request
91:     # We need this to close(as merged) merge requests that were merged into
92:     # target branch manually
93:     # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord
94:     def post_merge_manually_merged
95:       commit_ids = @commits.map(&:id)
96:       merge_requests = @project.merge_requests.opened
97:         .preload_project_and_latest_diff
98:         .preload_merge_data(@project)
99:         .preload_latest_diff_commit(@project)
100:         .where(target_branch: @push.branch_name).to_a
101:         .select(&:diff_head_commit)
102:         .select do |merge_request|
103:           commit_ids.include?(merge_request.diff_head_sha) &&
104:             merge_request.merge_request_diff.state != 'empty'
105:         end
106:       merge_requests = filter_merge_requests(merge_requests)
107: 
108:       return if merge_requests.empty?
109: 
110:       analyzer = Gitlab::BranchPushMergeCommitAnalyzer.new(
111:         @commits.reverse,
112:         relevant_commit_ids: merge_requests.map(&:diff_head_sha)
113:       )
114: 
115:       merge_requests.each do |merge_request|
116:         sha = analyzer.get_merge_commit(merge_request.diff_head_sha)
117:         merge_request.merge_commit_sha = sha
118:         merge_request.merged_commit_sha = sha
119: 
120:         # Look for a merged MR that includes the SHA to associate it with
121:         # the MR we're about to mark as merged.
122:         # Only the merged MRs without the event source would be considered
123:         # to avoid associating it with other MRs that we may have marked as merged here.
124:         source_merge_request = MergeRequestsFinder.new(
125:           @current_user,
126:           project_id: @project.id,
127:           merged_without_event_source: true,
128:           state: 'merged',
129:           sort: 'merged_at',
130:           commit_sha: sha
131:         ).execute.first
132: 
133:         source = source_merge_request || @project.commit(sha)
134: 
135:         MergeRequests::PostMergeService
136:           .new(project: merge_request.target_project, current_user: @current_user)
137:           .execute(merge_request, source)
138:       end
139:     end
140:     # rubocop: enable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord
141: 
142:     # Link LFS objects that exists in forks but does not exists in merge requests
143:     # target project
144:     def link_forks_lfs_objects
145:       return unless @push.branch_updated?
146: 
147:       merge_requests_for_forks.find_each do |mr|
148:         LinkLfsObjectsService
149:           .new(project: mr.target_project)
150:           .execute(mr, oldrev: @push.oldrev, newrev: @push.newrev)
151:       end
152:     end
153: 
154:     # Refresh merge request diff if we push to source or target branch of merge request
155:     # Note: we should update merge requests from forks too
156:     def reload_merge_requests
157:       merge_requests = @project.merge_requests.opened
158:         .by_source_or_target_branch(@push.branch_name)
159:         .preload_project_and_latest_diff
160:         .preload_merge_data(@project)
161: 
162:       merge_requests_from_forks = merge_requests_for_forks
163:         .preload_project_and_latest_diff
164:         .preload_merge_data(@project)
165: 
166:       merge_requests_array = merge_requests.to_a + merge_requests_from_forks.to_a
167:       filtered_merge_requests = filter_merge_requests(merge_requests_array)
168: 
169:       recheck_merge_requests_batched(filtered_merge_requests)
170: 
171:       # Upcoming method calls need the refreshed version of
172:       # @source_merge_requests diffs (for MergeRequest#commit_shas for instance).
173:       merge_requests_for_source_branch(reload: true)
174:     end
175: 
176:     def recheck_merge_requests_batched(filtered_merge_requests)
177:       source_branch_or_force_pushed_mrs = []
178:       all_mr_ids = []
179: 
180:       filtered_merge_requests.each do |merge_request|
181:         all_mr_ids << merge_request.id
182: 
183:         if branch_and_project_match?(merge_request) || @push.force_push?
184:           merge_request.reload_diff(current_user)
185:           schedule_duo_code_review(merge_request)
186:           source_branch_or_force_pushed_mrs << merge_request
187:         elsif merge_request.merge_request_diff.includes_any_commits?(push_commit_ids)
188:           merge_request.reload_diff(current_user)
189:         end
190:       end
191: 
192:       MergeRequest.batch_mark_as_unchecked(all_mr_ids, mrs_to_trigger: source_branch_or_force_pushed_mrs)
193: 
194:       # Clear existing merge error if the push were directed at the
195:       # source branch. Clearing the error when the target branch
196:       # changes will hide the error from the user.
197:       MergeRequest.batch_clear_merge_error(source_branch_or_force_pushed_mrs.map(&:id))
198: 
199:       enqueue_auto_merge_for_unchecked(filtered_merge_requests)
200:     end
201: 
202:     def enqueue_auto_merge_for_unchecked(merge_requests)
203:       auto_merge_mrs = merge_requests.select(&:auto_merge_enabled?)
204:       return if auto_merge_mrs.empty?
205: 
206:       auto_merge_mrs.first(AUTO_MERGE_BATCH_LIMIT).each_with_index do |mr, index|
207:         delay = index * AUTO_MERGE_DELAY_INTERVAL
208:         AutoMergeProcessWorker.perform_in(delay.seconds, { 'merge_request_id' => mr.id })
209:       end
210:     end
211: 
212:     def push_commit_ids
213:       @push_commit_ids ||= @commits.map(&:id)
214:     end
215: 
216:     def branch_and_project_match?(merge_request)
217:       merge_request.source_project == @project &&
218:         merge_request.source_branch == @push.branch_name
219:     end
220: 
221:     def outdate_suggestions(merge_request)
222:       outdate_service.execute(merge_request)
223:     end
224: 
225:     def outdate_service
226:       @outdate_service ||= Suggestions::OutdateService.new
227:     end
228: 
229:     def abort_auto_merges?(merge_request)
230:       merge_request.merge_params.with_indifferent_access[:sha] != @push.newrev
231:     end
232: 
233:     def abort_auto_merges(merge_request)
234:       return unless abort_auto_merges?(merge_request)
235: 
236:       learn_more_url = Rails.application.routes.url_helpers.help_page_url(
237:         'ci/pipelines/merge_trains.md',
238:         anchor: 'merge-request-dropped-from-the-merge-train'
239:       )

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module AutoMerge
4:   class BaseService < ::BaseService
5:     include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
6:     include MergeRequests::AssignsMergeParams
7: 
8:     def execute(merge_request)
9:       ApplicationRecord.transaction do
10:         register_auto_merge_parameters!(merge_request)
11:         yield if block_given?
12:       end
13: 
14:       notify(merge_request)
15:       AutoMergeProcessWorker.perform_async({ 'merge_request_id' => merge_request.id })
16: 
17:       strategy.to_sym
18:     rescue StandardError => e
19:       track_exception(e, merge_request)
20:       :failed
21:     end
22: 
23:     def process(_)
24:       raise NotImplementedError
25:     end
26: 
27:     def update(merge_request)
28:       assign_allowed_merge_params(merge_request, params.merge(auto_merge_strategy: strategy))
29: 
30:       return :failed unless merge_request.save
31: 
32:       strategy.to_sym
33:     end
34: 
35:     def cancel(merge_request)
36:       ApplicationRecord.transaction do
37:         clear_auto_merge_parameters!(merge_request)
38:         yield if block_given?
39:       end
40: 
41:       success
42:     rescue StandardError => e
43:       track_exception(e, merge_request)
44:       error("Can't cancel the automatic merge", 406)
45:     end
46: 
47:     def abort(merge_request, reason)
48:       ApplicationRecord.transaction do
49:         clear_auto_merge_parameters!(merge_request)
50:         yield if block_given?
51:       end
52: 
53:       success
54:     rescue StandardError => e
55:       track_exception(e, merge_request)
56:       error("Can't abort the automatic merge", 406)
57:     end
58: 
59:     def available_for?(merge_request)
60:       strong_memoize("available_for_#{merge_request.id}") do
61:         availability_details(merge_request).available?
62:       end
63:     end
64: 
65:     private
66: 
67:     def availability_details(merge_request)
68:       strong_memoize("availability_details_#{merge_request.id}") do
69:         unless merge_request.can_be_merged_by?(current_user)
70:           next AutoMerge::AvailabilityCheck.error(unavailable_reason: :forbidden)
71:         end
72: 
73:         mergeability_checks = merge_request.execute_merge_checks(
74:           MergeRequest.all_mergeability_checks,
75:           params: skippable_available_for_checks(merge_request),
76:           execute_all: false
77:         )
78: 
79:         unless mergeability_checks.success?
80:           next AutoMerge::AvailabilityCheck.error(unavailable_reason: :mergeability_checks_failed,
81:             unsuccessful_check: mergeability_checks.payload[:unsuccessful_check])
82:         end
83: 
84:         block_given? ? yield : AvailabilityCheck.success
85:       end
86:     end
87: 
88:     def skippable_available_for_checks(merge_request)
89:       merge_request.skipped_auto_merge_checks(
90:         auto_merge_strategy: strategy
91:       )
92:     end
93: 
94:     # Overridden in child classes
95:     def notify(merge_request); end
96: 
97:     def strategy
98:       strong_memoize(:strategy) do
99:         self.class.name.demodulize.remove('Service').underscore
100:       end
101:     end
102: 
103:     def register_auto_merge_parameters!(merge_request)
104:       assign_allowed_merge_params(merge_request, params.merge(auto_merge_strategy: strategy))
105:       merge_request.auto_merge_enabled = true
106:       merge_request.merge_user = current_user
107:       merge_request.save!
108:     end
109: 
110:     def clear_auto_merge_parameters!(merge_request)
111:       merge_request.auto_merge_enabled = false
112:       merge_request.merge_user = nil
113: 
114:       merge_request.clear_merge_params(clearable_auto_merge_parameters)
115: 
116:       merge_request.save!
117:     end
118: 
119:     # Overridden in EE child classes
120:     def clearable_auto_merge_parameters
121:       %w[
122:         should_remove_source_branch
123:         commit_message
124:         squash_commit_message
125:         auto_merge_strategy
126:       ]
127:     end
128: 
129:     def track_exception(error, merge_request)
130:       Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_exception(error, merge_request_id: merge_request&.id)
131:     end
132: 
133:     def logger
134:       @logger ||= Gitlab::AppLogger
135:     end
136:   end
137: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class AutoMergeService < BaseService
4:   include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
5: 
6:   STRATEGY_MERGE_WHEN_CHECKS_PASS = 'merge_when_checks_pass'
7:   # Only used in EE
8:   STRATEGY_ADD_TO_MERGE_TRAIN_WHEN_CHECKS_PASS = 'add_to_merge_train_when_checks_pass'
9:   STRATEGIES = [STRATEGY_MERGE_WHEN_CHECKS_PASS].freeze
10: 
11:   class << self
12:     def all_strategies_ordered_by_preference
13:       STRATEGIES
14:     end
15: 
16:     def get_service_class(strategy)
17:       return unless all_strategies_ordered_by_preference.include?(strategy)
18: 
19:       strategy_to_class_map[strategy]
20:     end
21: 
22:     def strategy_to_class_map
23:       {
24:         STRATEGY_MERGE_WHEN_CHECKS_PASS => AutoMerge::MergeWhenChecksPassService
25:       }
26:     end
27:   end
28: 
29:   def execute(merge_request, strategy = nil)
30:     strategy ||= preferred_strategy(merge_request)
31:     instance = auto_merge_service_instance(merge_request, strategy)
32: 
33:     return :failed unless instance&.available_for?(merge_request)
34: 
35:     instance.execute(merge_request)
36:   end
37: 
38:   def update(merge_request)
39:     return :failed unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
40: 
41:     perform_method(merge_request) do |instance|
42:       instance.update(merge_request)
43:     end
44:   end
45: 
46:   def process(merge_request)
47:     return unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
48: 
49:     perform_method(merge_request) do |instance|
50:       instance.process(merge_request)
51:     end
52:   end
53: 
54:   def cancel(merge_request)
55:     return error("Can't cancel the automatic merge", 406) unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
56: 
57:     perform_method(merge_request) do |instance|
58:       instance.cancel(merge_request)
59:     end
60:   end
61: 
62:   def abort(merge_request, reason)
63:     return error("Can't abort the automatic merge", 406) unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
64: 
65:     perform_method(merge_request) do |instance|
66:       instance.abort(merge_request, reason)
67:     end
68:   end
69: 
70:   def available_strategies(merge_request)
71:     self.class.all_strategies_ordered_by_preference.select do |strategy|
72:       auto_merge_service_instance(merge_request, strategy).available_for?(merge_request)
73:     end
74:   end
75: 
76:   def preferred_strategy(merge_request)
77:     available_strategies(merge_request).first
78:   end
79: 
80:   private
81: 
82:   def auto_merge_service_instance(merge_request, strategy)
83:     strong_memoize_with(:auto_merge_service_instance, merge_request, strategy) do
84:       self.class.get_service_class(strategy)&.new(project, current_user, params)
85:     end
86:   end
87: 
88:   def perform_method(merge_request)
89:     strategy = merge_request.auto_merge_strategy
90:     instance = auto_merge_service_instance(merge_request, strategy)
91: 
92:     if instance.present?
93:       yield(instance)
94:     else
95:       AutoMerge::BaseService.new(project, current_user, params).cancel(merge_request)
96:     end
97:   end
98: end
99: 
100: AutoMergeService.prepend_mod_with('AutoMergeService')

This audit maps the model contract, creation/update flows, every dependent area, permission/visibility guards, blast radius, and verification requirements.",

Shared abstractions live in app/models/concerns and lib/gitlab/.",
